Started with Acrosonic 938810.  Got the piano taken apart (gotta watch out for a couple cheek block screws I couldn't remove), vacuumed out the piano, including under the keyboard (removed keys), it was very dirty / dusty under there to where almost couldn't see the key framework, also windexed the keys.  Vacuumed out the piano, including the trapwork, although I still see a lot of debris sitting on the bridges under the strings, suspect blowing it out might clear it.
Removed action and put on table in cradle, then scotch-brited strings and brite boyed pedals (also lubricated) and pressure bar.  Also 🎣 some random things out of the action, started squaring some hammers, spacing also needs quite a bit of adjustment on some notes.  (I should probably have made a note which hammers weren't hitting all strings or adjacent notes before removing the action.)
The sticker pinning at the whippens is off center, so letting the stickers hang down isn't wise (there's a spot on the whippens where the travel is stopped in a way I think is not good to let it sit that way), so for now I have them resting on a stack of blocks.

Noticed on Young Chang G120584, Key 23 (G2) was stuck down a few mm. Have been doing tuning practice on Baldwin Acrosonic 938810 this week, the one I'm also refurbishing.  I still need to work on my temperament, checking with Tunelab (calibrated to this piano) after tuning showed about 1.5-3¢ off, worst being 3.5¢ and closest (but still unacceptable) was 0.1¢.
Also I need to work on stability and technique. Often when I play ff test blows, it'll drift 15+¢, and even when it seems stable I'll see it drift a cent or two.  I tuned A4 and the contiguous thirds Thursday, finished the rest of the temperament Friday, checked, and A4, which was no worse than 0.1¢ off right after tuning it, and it had spiked to +0.9¢.
I may try a tunelab tuning or two next week, and try to work on stability and technique.  (I feel if I can't get that under control, a perfect temperament won't do any good.)

Baldwin Acrosonic spinet #938810 - was having to "temporarily" set blow distance really close, like 1 ¼" to even begin to approach proper aftertouch. (Without any gauge, I could just get the samples to let off if I bore down on the key or played mf. Was advised to raise the keys to 21mm (I'd had them at 18.74mm or .718", they'd originally been around .65" above keyslip or so), so I did that and had to re-level the keys.
Also set the blow distance, took quite a bit of trial and error "dry-fitting" the felt shims during testing. (Was told a shorter blow distance was ok, the aftertouch would be regulated with the key dip. For now, one sample is ok, another is just a bit much and the third borderline not enough.)
Started with regulating lost motion, got almost through the bass section.  Was having trouble with 27 & 28, they'd be too much then too little without adjusting the capstan.  Found the end of the back rail cloth there was loose, so I glued it down.

Baldwin Acrosonic 938810 - finished regulating lost motion, regulated let off, aligned backchecks.
Since the key height was raised and blow distance closer, the key dip was very deep. As I was starting setting backcheck samples, one of them next to a blow distance sample, even though they checked the same distance, were about the thickness of the backcheck different.  Another sample was slapping against the drop sticker on release.
So, before continuing aligning backchecks, I put .060 and .020 punchings under every key so my dip wasn't so deep that I couldn't regulate the backcheck samples.  It's still somewhat deep, as so far I've put .045, .030 and another thin one under A0, B0, C1 so far when regulating dip.
Also helped a little with setting up Christmas stuff and PTG meeting Thursday, and refilled some front rail punchings in one of those boxes.

Cable 402514: did a full cleaning and vacuuming, cleaned strings with scotch brite and the entire soundboard with tsp, cleaned the dust off the bridges, cleaned tuning pins and in between, cleaned dampers, replaced felts on trapwork, left one pedal at Ben’s (the one with a hole), replaced felt bushings on lyre (missing 2), removed all hinges on music desk (it was a mess, full of holes, I think none of the hinges are original. Vacuumed keybed. Soda blasted action, reshaped all hammers. Reglued one loose hammer head. Will begin repining hammer flanges on Monday, it needs it desperately. 

Orders new parts to replace plastic on Knabe 143274 (backchecks, damper levers and flanges)

Tunings rescheduled: 3/5 (one of them didn’t schedule next tuning because he is seriously considering the rebuild, bass has some loose tuning pins)

Baldwin 29242: waiting for the 2 bass strings.
Gulbransen 191466: done
Steinway 113162: done

Extra stuff: Euphona: I helped and modified the slide door of the player.

M: M&H 22167 stringing progress, HD160 2026688 QC only needed minor damper work, Adam Schaaf 8051 needed some whippen repinning (broken flanges) and light voicing. Tu: Finished stringing up to bass section, lowest note broke at the beckett and needed a splice. The viennese action needed some repairs before getting taken out. W: Restrung bass section, pre-voiced, bored, and set up coving for hammers.
Kohler & Campbell 136433: tuned and voiced. Steinway 220857: filled screw holes, sanded, routing edges in progress.
All six Yamaha uprights are prepped and ready to go! I started the Ivers & Pond on Wednesday and will need all of next week to continue
Baldwin Acrosonic 1029243: tightened and lubricated action, reinstalled it. Yamahas for black Friday: qcd and polished them with Jake.

Hallet Davis 160 2026688, Tuning done. No issues found. Promberger 2263: Tuning done. Here the tuning pins seem to be a bit jumpy, especially towards the top end  of the piano. Hailun 121 296922, Tuning done. walnut HD 121 2274945, Tuning done. walnut HD 160 2173583, Tuning done. Hobart Cable T10078, Tuning done. Yamaha: 6144980, tuning done. Yamaha: 1811122, tuning done. Yamaha: 1630496 tuning done. Yamaha: 3162691, tuning done. Yamaha: 4465930, tuning done. Yamaha: 4404193, tuning done.
DuoArt mg12345: 2 of 3 main pin issues solved. Had to wait for new epoxy to arrive. Had to restore most upper auxiliaries due to brittle material.
Dara Salmond: needed to voice some notes and retune some unisons. Foster 49373: finished CAP, moving on to dampers. Will need all next week to work on DHRT. 6/6 Tunings Rescheduled.

Took off THU & FRI, Kranich & Bach 37121: finished restringing, chip tuned it up to approximate pitch, reassembled piano, note that the whole left side will need to be reglued, right now it is being held on by 4 screws (3 screws up through the key bed into the arms and 1 screw down thru the key bed into the bottom post, also I'm putting on the bottom board for extra support,
Cable 402514: blew out action, repined all hammer flanges . Tightened all screws. Tunings rescheduled: 0/1

Baldwin 29242: done, I installed the two pending bass strings and fixed the taller agraffe.
Steinway 113162: I started with the restrininging.
Extra stuff: I helped with the assembly of grey Ivers & Ponds, I made some modifications on the piano and one new piece for the fallboard system. 
I started helping Victoria with the backshop, orders and spreadsheets 



Photos: 60547 Steinway (after) // 96464 Steinway (before) // 17827 Bush & Lane (before) // 38186 Merril (before) // 1077 Schimmel (before) // 1510185 Kawai (before) //

M: Hailun 258357, didn't know there were plans to do heavier servicing so I did a fair bit. Bedded keyframe, fixed letoff, redid hammer line, dip to aftertouch, tightened plate/case screws causing clicks, dusted/polished case and interior, fixed some ticking/ringing dampers. W: 5P 260339 didn't need much, tightened loose plate screws with bushings, gave it some aftertouch, fixed a pedal squeak. Th: Vineyard appt went well, she said she didn't touch anything and that the washers just fell out but that's physically impossible. Nothing at all was broken and the allen key keeping the sustain lever has a ton of friction so it wouldn't fall out naturally. When I arrived the allen key was in the brackets but the lever wasn't, which is also physically impossible if the allen key hadn't been removed. Either way everything was fixed and she was happy when I left. M&H 22167: Finished hammer prep, hung them, started regulation. Keys needed chucking treatment, some bushings had fallen out/were way too short and were replaced. Keys are level, hammers are aligned to strings, and whippens are in the process of being travelled. The back action will need a fair bit of servicing since the underlevers are glued and everything is dirty/tight. I'm hoping for about 3 days more of regulation and 1-2 for damper work.
got all but 3 hammers glued in ( the 3 i damaged the shanks:(  i messed up alot so i to re put a good amount them back in. currently squaring and spacing the hammers
Steinway 220857: fixed new cracks under bass bridge that popped up when working on it. finished shaping edge of bridges. Sounds really good with plate in. Downbearing in progress.
Ivers & Pond 17292: In progress. I just need two more days to finish

Bubinga Pramberger J00020603 (269): tightened upstop rail. 

Hardman 92975 (115): raised the hammer line on 2 sections to introduce after touch. The top section just had 2 outliers, so I did those with dip. Fixed the sostenuto holding up dampers when it wasn't supposed to. Filled a stripped cheek block screw hole with wood glue. 

Hailun 121 296922 (150): new piano prep (again).

Baldwin Acrosonic 1029243 (104): fixed lost motion to put the hammers on the rest rail. F#5 put spring back under jack. Adjusted pedals. Bent the action bracket (back to where it was, probably) out of the way of the left pedal stick. Fixed a couple dampers that weren't dampening. Fixed the let-off on notes that were blocking. Technically all the notes work. It could use a whole re-regulation, but I didn't spend the time. I tightened the existing screws on the music desk and it seemed okay to me, let me know if it needs more. 

walnut Hallet Davis 160 2173583 (145): new piano prep

Hailun 151 287741 (100): fixed the key slip, but a couple notes are still sticking. That's as far as I got.

Yamaha C7 2750092, Tuning done. Yamaha U3 1644386, Tuning done. Yamaha 4294142, Tuning done. Boston B111144, Tuning done. (Repetition on F#5 seems off when sustain pedal is depressed.) Hailun 5P 260339, Tuning done. Hailun 151 287741, Tuning done. (This piano is in some need of regulation, hammers are out of alignment in the mid section mostly, and notes D4 and A4 aren't returning most likely due to hammer alignment issues.) Steinway 234553, Tuning done. Hailun 178 Grand 287342, Tuning done. Hailun 5P 239086, Tuning done. Mason & Hamlin 37957, Tuning done. Replaced some lights in the main shop that were mismatched in the chandeliers, as well as the two tracks on the far east end of the main shop. I plan on replacing every warm toned bulb with a white toned bulb. I also masked the east wall and painted that wall in the Hydn room, I'll need to most likely move each piano out of the room to go over the remainder. The last piece of flooring was installed in the attic for more floor space. I also polished the piano's with Cory High Gloss on the balcony area, and vacuumed the balcony area, as well as the recital hall. I also replaced the doorknob to the napping room with a coded door knob.

Monday 12/02/24. CABLE NELSON 186573. Carefully removed ivory keyTOPS salvaging 80%, then installed all new bushings. Installed all new white keytops with elastic.
Tuesday 12/03/24. Ran through router 3 and 4, sanded all Sharps and sent to paint shop. 
Wednesday 12/04/24. Finished CABLE NELSON project.
Wellington 155111. Installed all keys on pin board and applied baking soda, then numbered all keys. Removed all old bushings. 
Thursday 12/05/24. Removed all white keytops, then installed all new bushings. 
Friday 12/06/24. Installed all new white keytops with elastics. Removed all old sharps and cut out new plastic sharps.

DuoArt mg12345: 17 pins redone. 1 string replacement. Casters. 2 tunings. It seems stable. As a reminder we had talked about someone else doing regulation. It's ready. Autopiano 185472: 40 striker plates prepped.

Foster 49373: Jake found a bag of shanks so hammers are glued into shanks and I’ll be ready to install them on Monday. Will need all next week to continue this. 2 Grand Refurbs: started the Yamaha GH1 277632. Almost done with the cleaning portion. I can work on this while waiting for glue to dry when installing hammers on the Foster. Linda Knudsen Warranty: had to repin a jack. 10/10 tunings rescheduled.
Gulbransen 170677: I've finished restoring all the parts in the rotating motor, next I'm gonna make the new strikers and the new, although we are going to need more of the really thin leather

Kranich & Bach 37121: reassembled trapwork and got it working with some tweaking and replacing some missing parts, there are 2 more screws up through the bottom board that will have to be removed to reglue the side of piano, I replaced a few back checks that had broken off,a dozen or so of double flanges cracked, and at first I replaced them with Steinway flanges (which worked with a little modification; however, a decided that everything was not lining up properly, and so I required many of 3D printed flanges, rebushing all of them was the most rebushing I've done, so I gained some skill by the end, same story for the whippen flanges I needed 3D printed (and they all work wonderfully but getting it on the damper stem with the little spring is probably the hardest thing,and I'm still trying to install them,  I was embarrassed that so many of the jack flanges that I had glued didn't line up with hammer butts, but after I learned to align the back checks first then the regluing for the second time got the jacks centered properly, I asked Jake to look at the whippen, and he identified 10 or so that were too wiggly, I repinned them.

Cable 402514: traveled hammers, lubricated knuckles, leveled keys. 

Adam Schaaf: moved to this project, piano is being delivered on Wednesday. Will replace 9 ivory heads. Already found matching new ones. Will glue them on Monday. 

Tunings rescheduled: 4/4

Steinway 113162: I started restringing the bass section. 
Extra stuff: I started helping Victoria with my new duties, created and organized spreadsheets and spent a lot of time in the back shop this week. 

Photos: I took photos of about 10 pianos this week but I need to check the serial numbers to upload them.

All calendar items completed (S&S 60547, K&B 38694). The M&H 22167 is not done yet, though the first regulation pass was completed Friday. If it can't wait until I get back the only important thing is that the back action gets soda blasted and springs checked/lubricated as necessary. They are hide glued to a rail, but I couldn't break any off so it should be fine. The blocks that the back action rotates on need to be rebushed and well lubricated. WELLINGTON: 155111 bridges have not been redone, all of them have negative bearing. Bass apron has been cleaned and re-glued/screwed on to the soundboard. S&S 234553: I didn't do much of a QC pass since I didn't have pedals. I checked for and eliminated a few more guide rail clicks and wouldn't be surprised if a few more pop up down the road. The keytops were fixed and received Victoria's approval.

Steinway 234553: completed voicing, cleaned a little, raised hammer line on very top section only (had very little after touch) touched up a few little cosmetic flaws with sharpie. Steinway 220857: A broken agraffe gave me trouble - broke a screw extractor inside it. Ended up drilling out and repairing. Down bearing set, removed top material. Next will drill holes and chisel.
Ivers & Pond 17292: Done! Duo/Art: Done! Mason & Hamlin 68655: QC is done! Ready to move on to the next piano.

Hailun 151 287741 (100): eased keys. Aeolian 161670: QC. D#3 had a bass string replaced with the wrong length, so it's going to sound out of tune forever. I muted it. The whole tune seems more unstable than usual. Eased keys, shimmed up stop rail, fixed a couple dampers, adjusted and lubricated pedals, put rubber bumpers on music desk. Hailun 148 QRS 2787651 (126): this one is actually a Hallet Davis. Shimmed the key slip. Hailun 5P 239086 (137): C#3 damper click fixed.

Steinway 60547, Tuning done. Aeolian 161670, Tuning done. Kranich & Bach 38694, Tuning done. Hailun HU-1P 289475, Tuning done. walnut HD 121 2274945, Tuning done, needs regulation. Hailun V126 V86850, Tuning done. Mason & Hamlin 68655, Tuning done. silent play Hailun 5P 260226, Tuning done. white Hailun 121 272834, Tuning done. Nickel Plate Knabe IKQFG0036, Tuning done. Finished painting the Hydn room, and filled up the red van with fuel, and clipped the zip ties holding the seatbelts. I also went and picked up some lacquer from Sherwin Williams for the refinishers. Painted the walls underneath the stairs in the middle shop. Replaced some off colored bulbs in the main shop, I'll finish that next week.

Monday 12/09/24. Wellington 155111: Router 3 and 4 on new white key tops up to key 51.
Tuesday 12/10/24. Finished Wellington 155111. WURLITZER 983993, placed keys on pin board and apply baking soda, then numbered all keys.
Wednesday 12/11/24. Removed all old bushings and installed new bushings up to key 42.
Thursday 12/12/24. Finished installing new bushings on all keys, sanded all white keys up to key 42 with 4 fine grit sandpaper.
Friday 12/13/24. Finished sanding all white keys then polished all keys. Cleaned all sharps as best as possible. I finished WURLITZER project 983993. I finished FOSTER project 49373 a few weeks ago.

Autopiano 185472: this stack is only 2 decks of 40 strikers. I have completely finished rebuilding 1 deck. The other deck is prepped including the striker plates. Kawai 1510185: reglued sides and thoroughly inspected.
Mike Earnhart: just retuned some of the bass. Foster 49373: finished hammers. Started regulation. Will need two more days to finish this project. Rescheduled 9/9 tunings.

Gulbransen: wind motor needed some touches but now is working well. All the upper part of the player is mostly reassembled, Last thing I was working on was making all the new strikers and I made 62 out of 88 before we ran out of the wood we use for the strikers. I installed the genio system on the Hallett Davis and there was a bass string broken. I did go to the house of the euphona too and fortunately the piano was working well, it's just the costumer didn't know how to pedal.

Flew in Tuesday evening from CA, returned to BLP Wednesday.

Colby 30776 - started teardown for full rebuild. Disassembled and stored cabinetry, could not see top of pinblock on account of a glued-on wood strip.  Suspect pinblock problems: many strings are an octave of more flat, and several tuning pins felt quite loose (a little looser than a few in a piano I tuned at a store almost 20 years ago), and the torque wrench barely moved at all (maximum maybe 10-20 or so).

There was a lot of 🐀 evidence in the piano, and even a 🏴‍☠️🩻 in the bottom left.  I used one of the larger red shopvacs to clean it up.

Am gluing bottom board, was cracked along about 85% of its length.  Soundboard has lots of major cracks (and offsets), bridges are split as well (with some bridge pins in upper bass and high treble in the wrong places), and bass apron is cracked.

Have gotten mostly through cleaning up trap work, other than a few parts (like the brackets between the pedals) not taking well to buffing, and some of the wood bushings through those are cracked.

Casters are removed, need to replace them (temporarily in bags for each one with screws, es viernes a las cuatro de la tarde).

Kranich & Bach 37121: my goal this week was to install dampers, numerous repairs were necessary as things kept breaking as I was aligning the damper heads, it's finally ready to glue the dampers, I'm currently trying to find enough unichord  bass dampers,okay so now I'm making my own monochord dampers using the bi-chords and the raw mono felt,

Adam Schaff: finished replacing ivories, QC’d, done. 

Cable 402514: installed action on keybed, had to replace 2 useless screws. Aligned hammers to strings, spaced and squared a couple of hammers. Began regulating section ends, on Wednesday I noticed a lot of chucking keys so at the end of the day I asked Marcelo to fix them over night.  Most were fixed by Thursday but some needed some extra glue and I found like 4 others that I hadn’t noticed, asked him to fix them while I was tuning and he did. I didn’t get to work on my piano after tuning because I got back late and then had a meeting. All chucking keys are fixed and back on the keybed. Will continue regulating on Monday. 

Tunings rescheduled: 4/4

Steinway 113162: done, I left some bass pins with extra torches. I installed the sides and arms 
Ludwig & Co: I’m started CAP, I’m working in the “trap work” now, looking for a match pedals but I thinking in drilling new holes and install 3 new pedals. 
Extra stuff: helping the refinishing shop.

Photos: 1029243 Baldwin (after) // T10078 Hobart & Cable (after) // FAKE111 Ivers & Pond (after) // 117260 Steinway (before) // 68655 Mason and Hamlin (after) // 980254 Kawai (after)
Steinway 220857: drilled bridge pin holes, applied dag, chiseling about halfway done.
Knabe 143274: Almost done with replacing the plastic portion. I just need part of Monday to finish that and then I can do the refurbish. I will need all of next week.

Taylor 23325: bored hammers, put shanks on 2 sections and hung 1 section. My guess is by the end of Monday they'll all be hung, and then someone can pick up with squaring and spacing them. Steinway 234553: installed all the replated hardware, QC. Hailun 151 287741: QC for delivery. The music desk is hard to slide because the factory installed the left rail crooked.

Kawai KG-2C 980254, Tuning done. HD 121 2739386, Tuning done. Steinway model O 201071, Tuning done. Yamaha C3 3250506, Tuning done. Kawai 152152, Tuning done. Ivers & Pond 17292, Tuning done. Hailun 121 296922, Tuning done. Yamaha U1 4249567, Tuning done. Hailun 178 313357, Tuning done. I painted the east walll of the middle shop.and installed a new fan in the men's restroom. I also replaced a couple of burnt out lightbulbs within the main sales floor.

Monday 12/16/24. Kimball 176979: placed all keys on pin board and applied baking soda. Removed all old bushings, then installed new bushings up to key 37.
Tuesday 12/17/24. Finished installing all bushings, then removed all white key tops and router 2 all key heads, then rubber band keytops up to key 45.
Wednesday 12/18/24. Finished installing new white key tops, then router 3 and 4 up to key 56.
Thursday 12/19/24. Finished project kimball 176979. Baldwin 29242, carefully cleaned all keys, Victoria already worked on these ivory tops, removed all old bushings up to key 47. 
Friday 11/20/24. Installed the new bushings up to key 43.

Built drying racks. DuoArt mg12345: reassembled player, identified issues - tracking and motor. Motor rebuilt and seems in correct order. The tracking valve block has been rebuilt and will be tested Monday morning.

Alisha West: took care of a noisy key and retuned a couple unisons. Foster 49373: had quite a bit of family things this week so I’m still doing regulation. I probably need about 4 more hours to finish this project. 2 Grand Referbs: did not get to these this week. 3/7 tunings rescheduled.
Gulbransen I've made all the pneumatic strikers from the wood Matthew got, and I've glued all the hinges for the strikers. I'm currently gluing the cloth to all the strikers

Colby 30776 – bottom board crack glued, finished cleaning up trapwork.  Pedals are in a bag, but still downstairs with the box, as we’re now sending plate screws up and the strings are still in the piano.
Replaced casters.  The front toe blocks came off with the front bar with the pedal openings, so I put the casters on there out of the piano.  When the piano is tipped back up it’ll need approximately 2” or so blocks under the front sides of the piano. (I was unable to start destringing, or remove the plate, I’m not Samson so I can’t just lift the entire assembly out of the piano.)
Keybed has been removed, took a lot to clean the mouse evidence and there’s still some left (even after using almost an entire bag of soda) but it is a little better.  Polished some balance rail pins (had to use 100, 220, 400 sandpaper as the scotch-brite wasn’t doing anything) from note 88 to note 20.
Have taken and uploaded quite a few pictures, including the string felt configuration (also including hitch pins, there are several where one string loops around two hitch pins.)

Kranich & Bach 37121: so I made the mono bass dampers, but when in the piano the felts were too wide, but Jake helped me find the narrow ones, and after making the dampers they actually worked, the other dampers went well, however, when doing the damper timing several damper levers broke, this has been the case all week that things keep breaking even when I'm bending the wires being extremely delicate, using the premium wood glue actually fixed breaks, I couldn't find enough salvage metal stems for the damper levers and had to use the best (longest) I could find, now I'm trying to do damper timing delicately, I missed a day this week,

Cable 402514: I’m regulating drop on all of the notes. 

Steinway model O 201071: fixed all buzzes, did QC, touched up a spot in the plate that had scratched off (unnoticeable now), cleaned the dust off the plate and cleaned the piano with Cory pink polish. 

I went through job applications on Thursday and also helped fix a fallboard before that. Today I did interviews all day. 

Tunings rescheduled: 5/5

Ludwig & Co: trap work done, I’m working on the wheels but someone till up my piano and broke a important part of the caster area (wooden block was push in and pieces of wood broken off) I’m repairing this now with glue and extras screws. 

Photos: Howard 316299 (after)
Ivers & Pond 17292: completed voicing. Young Chang G077818: completed voicing. Steinway 220857: finished chiseling, sanded. Bridge pins in progress. Will probably need new project next week.

Bush and lane: 17827
Began disassembly 
Removed face, top, keys, action,pillars and bottom.
Saw that bottom had a substantial crack in it.
Removed pedal hardware and began to build a replacement 10 3/4 x 59 5/16
Ludwig 23086 Started tear down Ended with soda blasting pedal board
Knabe 143274: Almost done. I just need Monday to finish. I will be taking New Year's Eve and New Year's Day off next week.
Taylor 23325: finished hanging hammers. Started aligning them. A couple of them need to be traveled, the front rail pins need to be aligned, and one of the whippens is loose.

Schafer & Sons 6072606, Tuning done. Young Chang 107 43936, Tuning done. white Hailun 151 258529, Tuning done. (Note: Decal on the fallboard is not in perfect alignment, and I my opinion should be redone.) Young Chang G077818, Tuning done. Yamaha U2 980265, Tuning done. Adjusted the mute rail to properly mute the piano when pedal is locked. HD 148 2705965, Tuning done. I had painted Alisa's office, might need to do a second coat on the wall that was blue, I'll check on it on Monday, and if I determine it needs it, I'll go over that particular wall again so it all looks pristine!

Monday 12/23/24. Finished installing new bushings for BALDWIN project 29242, then sanded all Sharps and sended to paint shop. SMITH & NIXON 20757, placed keys on pin board and applied baking soda, then numbered all keys. Removed all old bushings and installed new bushings up to 23.
Tuesday 12/24/24. Finished installing new bushings, router 2 all key heads then installed all key tops with elastic.
Wednesday 12/25/24. CHRISTMAS DAY.
Thursday 12/26/24. Router 3 and 4 all white keys, then sanded all Sharps and sended to paint shop. I finished the SMITH&NIXON 20757 project.
Friday 12/27/24. STRICH ZEIDLER 4261. I placed the keys on the board and applied baking soda, then numbered all the keys, then removed all the old bushings and installed the new bushings.

DuoArt mg12345: I tried for a day and a half to get the tracking valve block working right. I think whoever refurbished it last changed something. IDK. Nacho took it for a few and also couldn't figure it out. I will call in Connor if that's the next best step. Otherwise I will return to the Autopiano for now. I have the flu.

Sharon Cleveland: still had issues with a squeaky pedal that I fixed at the tuning. I just had to lubricate part of the trap work that I didn’t lubricate the first time. Kylee Matson: one key was physically sticking which was a bushing problem. I eased all the bushings and raised the hammer line and the touch felt a little lighter to me. Yamaha GH1 277632: just finished with repinning the hammers and will need another two days to finish this one. 2015 HD 160 1554743: did not get to this one yet. 5/6 tunings rescheduled.

Gulbransen: strikers as their sandalone are ready, I need to glued them all to the stack . Friday: I went to the costumers house to fix the supposed pedal problem, but there was no pedal problem, instead the whole qrs system wasn't working properly
Kranich & Bach 37121: finished up with dampers, measured, bored hammers, as of now I've glued the shanks on the bass hammers,

Cable 402514: I’m refining hammer height with fallboard and keyslip on all hammers (for some reason I did each regulation step on the entire piano instead of each section…I won’t forget next time). 

Steinway model O 201071: made let off sooner and lowered drop, made sure all hammers were checking at a very slight touch (pianisisisimo). 

Tunings rescheduled: 0/1 (because she might be getting a refurb with us before a year)

Steinway 220857: Size 8 bridge pins have been on back order, should get here mid to late January. Finished everything except the size 8 pins that need to go in bass bridge. That will be fast when they get here. Kimball 316512 (75): Ready to start. -- helped train, moved machine into kitchen.

Summary: Wing 39450-finish 100% ( without Dical) . Weber 43336- finish lacquered soundboard.  Kimball 327997- polish only piano 95%.  Howard 156792- put Dical , full assembly 100%.

Summary: Steinway 214896 - 100%, Kohler & Campbell 136433 - 100%, Kimball 562932 - 100%, Strich & Zeidler 4261 - 100%, Emerson 101012 - 20%, Kimball 531140 - 20%, Duo Art 11612345 -10%.

Ludwig 23086
Finished the trapwork
Replaced and leveled casters
Removed key board and cleaned
Measured bass strings
Started removing bass strings
Knabe 143274: Done! Baldwin 29242: In progress I will need all of next week to continue.

Schaeffer 43165, Tuning done, no issues felt at the present time. Samick 8510474, Tuning done. A couple of notes closest to the lower bass end (most likely within the first six notes, feels kind of jumpy/creaky when tuning it, it otherwise holds well, just feels jumpy/creaky. Hailun 178 313793, Tuning done, holds well, no issues. Hailun 151 287446, Tuning done, no issues found. Yamaha        U3H 1644980, tuning was completed, and I repinned the notes A7, and G3 I believe. Regulation was great, no issues. Yamaha T137829, Tuning done, also required a pitch raise. Mason & Hamlin 68655, tuning done. Hailun 121 277640, Tuning done, definitely needs a through regulation, special attention to F3, and G3, I believe the front rail bushing is too tight and needs to be regulated. Knabe 143274, Tuning done. Pitch Raise done. No issues found. Yamaha U3 1630946, Tuning done. F4, F#4, and B4 have some clicking of some sort in each note. Applied another coat of paint to the south wall of Alisa's office. Replaced the bulbs to match in the chandeliers in the main shop. I ordered new recessed lights so I could replace the ones in the main shop with white lights. Also sanded down some spots that I'd fixed with DryDex in the secret shop, as well as in the storage room at the top of the stairs by the attic. I'll start moving pianos around in that storage room to mask, prep, and  paint the walls this week.

Monday 12/30/24. STRICH ZEIDER 4261. Installed all new bushings in and removed all old keytops and installed new keytops with elastic up to 44.
Tuesday 12/31/24. Finished installing all keytops and ran all new keys through router 3 and 4.
Wednesday 01/01/25 holiday new year.
Thursday 01/02/25. Finished STRICH 4261 project. STEINWAY 113162, installed keys on board and applied baking soda, then numbered all keys, then removed all old bushings.
Friday 01/03/25. Removed all ivory from keys and ran all key heads through router 2, installed all new bushings up to key 62

Autopiano 185472: second half of strikers finished and glued back on deck.     Note- Connor was sick so he will be in next week to troubleshoot the tracker. I asked Dorris to hold off on the refinishing.
Yamaha GH1 277632: Refurb completed. 2015 HD 160 1554743: just started this today and will need the rest of next week to work on it.

A short week Kranich & Bach 37121:  Set the hammer height and cut the shanks in the treble sections, it was difficult to set, because the rest rail doesn't move, so I had to be creative to push the hammers up against the strings, and making both sections perfectly level, the bass section has a problem, I had used the the triple dampers, and the hammers sat high in order to clear the dampers, but that put the strike point so the hammers don't hit their strings, I will have to redo the bass dampers so that the dampers are flush with the top of the damper heads, then I can set the bass hammers at a good height,

Yamaha U1 2371222: completed voicing. Kimball 316512: Fixed middle and bass sections, will need to refine, but everything is hitting the right string. Top section needs redone. Cut shanks and redrilled holes.

Ludwig 23086
Removed all Strings
Removed tuning pins
Removed plate
Cleaned soundboard 
Replaced and realigned new hammer butts
Baldwin 29242: Still in progress. I've had setbacks with this piano so I will need until about Thursday to finish. All other showroom items are also complete.

Hobart Cable T10078, Tuning done. Hailun 151 287446, Tuning done. Hailun 161 258653, Tuning done. Everett 248443, Tuning done. Hailun 178 287342: removed all metal hardware for replating. Kimball 143998, Tuning done. Kawai GM-10 F081630, Tuning done. Harrington 108453: strings and tuning pins removed. Hailun 5P 239086, Tuning done. Hallet Davis 188 2788360, Tuning done. Rodgers Organ, dusted and cleaned. Yamaha C7 2750092, Tuning done, piano has been polished. Yamaha GH1 277632, Tuning done. Under the stairs was cleaned out, and wood cut down into as many 12.5"x39" slats as possible. Benches put together. Christmas has been taken down and put away.

Autopiano 185472:striker decks refashioned after many leak problems. Turns out the old wood is more porous than you assume. Almost done regulating the stack. Initial tests very solid.  DuoArt mg12345: I returned the piano back to the main shop and asked sanders to wait until after Connor came in - which he did Thursday. He gave me a few ideas. I'm in Saturday to apply his ideas.               Note: a few of the new hires seem to feel comfortable asking for my help so I have been somewhat pulled away. I don't mind if you don't.

White Hailun 121 272834: QC complete. There was a small loose dowel piece between the soundboard and plate that was causing buzzing and took a while to get out. Lynette Fletcher: just had to retune some notes in the highest octave that were bothering her. Yamaha GH1 277632: aftertouch refined and key slip shimmed. Heather Cheney: had to lower the upstop rail to get rid of clicking. She originally noticed the clicking at the tuning appointment recently and I had only fixed some loose hammers that were also clicking. 2015 HD 160 1554743: just about to start regulation so I’ll need about three more days to finish this. 5/8 tunings scheduled for next year.

Kranich & Bach 37121:  After our conversation I consulted with Victoria on the best way to do each step, I have hanged the hammers, before aligning them to the strings Victoria helped identify 57 hammer butts that need to be re-pinned (and possibly re-bushed) I have started that process, also I tuned a Yamaha U1,
Yamaha GH1 277632: completed voicing. Kimball 316512: rehung hammers (still needs straightening touch up). Gave it a chip tuning. Will get started on regulation next week.

Ludwig 23086
Reglued and reinstalled all jack flanges
Replaced let off buttons and eye screws
Installed let off rail
Lubricated action
Replaced rest rail cloth and polished metal
Attached bridle straps
Replaced spring rail felt and put everything back in storage
AutoPiano 99849
Installed Plate
Put on new plate felt
Started restringing
Baldwin 29242: Done! Heintzman 27802: In progress. I will need all of next week to do this piano.

Behning 22359, C#2 hammer, and D2 hammers need to be realigned. G#4, and A4 hammers are catching, and need to be realigned. E5 broken string, A#5 hammer won't strike the strings.B5 feels too light, and clicks, A6 feels pretty stiff and it's hammer isn't striking the string. C#7 R, and D7 string broken. D#7 string broken. F7, F#7, G7 hammers need realigned. A7 string broken. C7 string broken. Treble bridge is cracked near the top beyond the strut, between where string D#4, and E4 cross the bridge, plate is missing three bolts, top Bass side corner, and the treble corner. F1 seems to be blocked, and won't strike the strings. Hailun HU-5P 130304, Needs retuning, pitch raised twice. Hailun HU-5P 130604, Tuning done. Gebruder Schulz 824003, Tuning done. Steinway 214896, Tuning done. walnut HD 160 2173583, Tuning done. Hailun 178 313793, Tuning done. Strich & Zeidler 4261, Tuning done, it'll need regulation. walnut Hallet Davis 121 2798056, Tuning done. Hallet Davis QRS 148 2788020, Tuning done. Hailun 151 287446, Tuning done. I painted more of the east wall of the upper storage room above the stairs. I put the boxes near the dumpster in the dumpster. Put the MDF against the wall outside and covered it with a tarp! I also cut and put away the metal pallet from the CNC machine. I picked up lacquer and sealer for Julio from Sherwin-Williams.

DuoArt mg12345: tracker is working finally. This player is close. Once it come out of coating I will need a day to button it up. Autopiano 185472: striker stack complete and regulated. Have started top end: polishing etc. maybe one more day on that. I haven't even looked at the feeders or auxiliaries yet so I can't give a timeline.

Lacey Bangerter: had some creaking when pedal was played and I tightened a lot of screws around the bottom half of the piano and it was a little better. I said the piano may need to settle and see if it’s still creaking in a month or two. It also had a pedal squeak on the sustain pedal. I took the pedal components completely apart and lubricated the moving parts. That seemed to work and she tested out the piano afterward and it was good. A little later when I was back at the shop I guess it started squeaking again. Melissa was going to figure out who to send and when. Behning 22359: I took care of all the keys that weren’t working at all and got them to function. There are several treble strings that are broken and I didn’t take care of those. I can if you’d like me to or I can just remove the broken strings. I also noticed the arm that connects the sustain pedal to the rod that pushes up the dampers is missing so the pedal doesn’t work. 2015 HD 160 1554743: Refurb completed. Schafer & Sons 6’9”: cleaning in progress. Will probably finish the whole project by next Friday. Tunings scheduled a year out: 7/8

Kranich & Bach 37121: I need to finish aligning hammers to strings and then regulation, I don't know what to say, but I've tried my absolute best with this piano, but it's beyond me, I need to pass it on to somebody else, the level of difficulty on this piano is way beyond my skill level,

Kimball 316512: Did some repairs and prep on action that was missed. Touched up hammer straightening. Leveled keys, regulation in progress. **The keys haven't been done yet - the original keys are kind of warped on top, so there might be more leveling touch up than normal after the keys are done.

Nordiska Grand DG00831, Tuning done. HD 170 2705197, Tuning done. walnut Hailun French leg 121 278040, Tuning done. walnut Hailun 151 316140, Tuning done. walnut Hailun 121 322866, Tuning done. I painted some more in the upper piano storage room. I also painted and fixed some holes in the wall in the show room near the ramp. Broke down and threw away the boxes overflowing near the dumpster. Broke down a desk for Ben. I went to Lowe's and got some more paint trays, rollers, as well as a drain trap O-ring to fix Julio's leaking sink! Did some replanting work for Ben, well buffing at this point. Love the idea of learning how to chemically replate.

Thursday 01/23/24. MERRIL 38180. I put all the keys on the board and soda blasted, then numbered all the keys. Removed all the old bushings and put in the new bushings up to key 70.
Friday 01/24/25. I finished installing the new bushings, then corrected all the key tops and polished them. I sanded all the sharps with acetone and delivered them to the paint shop.

Autopiano 185472: Top end is finished except- I have to see if f I can rebuild a valve. It's hard to find one that's not $350. Feeder bellows is hours from completion. Only one Aux on this player and i also rebuilt it. I will start reassembly Monday. Tunings 1/2 rescheduled.

Lacey Bangerter: used the weakest spring and was able to stretch it out to size. David Alger: thought the fallboard on his piano was stuck open but we discovered that the knobs were decorative and it had no fallboard. Brittlyn Doyle: replaced a broken/missing hammer with new shank and a new hammer. Heintzman 27802: QC done. Still needs “After” pictures. I noticed one white key that was lower than the rest after I put it all together. I can take a look at it on Monday. There were several jacks that were stiff that I repinned. Schafer & Sons 6’9”: still working on this. Just about to look over the regulation. Will need two more days I think to finish this. Tunings scheduled for next year: 4/8

Hailun 130304:  The serial number here is incorrect, the actual serial number is 130304, tightened all plate screws I could reach, finished refurbishing (I think), went to 4 tunings, just got started on Kawai KG-2C 980254,

Steinway 220857: Finished up bass bridge pins with size 9 instead of the size 8 on backorder. I noticed another crack appear on the sound board, expanded from the end of a shim. Kimball 316512: regulating aftertouch in progress. Need to do damper, spoons and pedals.

Painted more of the storage room by the stairs on the west side of the building. I'll replace all the lights to match next week and continue painting.

Monday 01/27/25. ACROSONIC 475341. Completed all schedule requirements. MERRIL 38180: Completed project.
Tuesday 01/28/25. Router 2 and 3 all keytops, then sanded all sharps and delivered to paint shop.
Wednesday 01/29/25. Completed project STEINWAY 113162.
SOHMER 220644 disassembled keyboard and removed all keys from piano. Numbered all keys.
Thursday 01/30/25. Removed old key tops and installed new key tops up to key 68.
Friday 01/31/25. Completed installing key tops and router all new key tops 3 and 4, then cleaned all sharps. Installed all keys on the piano. 
Completed SOHMER project 220644

Autopiano 185472: feeders complete. Valve rebuilt. Regulation done. Reassembly started. It's hard to say a timeline but I hope to start testing by Wednesday. Shattuck 16460: QC nearly complete - may come in Saturday afternoon to finish. Maybe an hour r two.

Renea Pelo: fixed a sticking key that I didn’t find during the tuning. Briana Jones: corrected some unisons. Shelly Jaynes: did a full pinblock treatment and a tuning the next day. Had to pound in some bass pins that were still loose. Acrosonic 475341: soft QC complete. Jon Laudie: one bass hammer was rubbing against its neighbor and a treble note had some lost motion that was causing an inconsistent strike. Schafer & Sons 6’9”: needed a bit more work and I didn’t had as much time in the shop so I need one more day. Just need to fix some damper timing and adjust the pedals. Did not get to the PRSB list yet. 6/8 tunings rescheduled.

Did 7 tunings this week, and my times are improving, Kawai KG-2C 980254: thorough cleaning, shined up the very rusty strings, buffed plate screws, i Brite Boyed the pedals (I wasn't sure if you wanted me to buff them super bright), I reshaped the hammers, and Victoria is training me on bolstering the knuckles,

Kranich & Bach 37121 (71): Missing keys, did not voice. Merrill 38180 (69): Kaitlyn was not done yet, did not voice. *completed all 6 other voicings. Steinway 220857: Fixed crack. Kimball 316512: Keys are ready. Found the back rail had debris underneath I hadn't noticed before. Had to clear out and reglue, so there was a good amount of fixing with key leveling. Leveling in progress. Might be able to finish regulation next week.

Finished Restringing
Glued left leg back on
Kurtzmann 95827
Started making a new Pin block
Roughed in general shape
Started to drill holes for plate screws
Autopiano 99849: Done! Ready to move on to the next piano.

Kohler & Campbell 136433, Tuning done. Nickel Plate Knabe IKQFG0036, Tuning done. Ivers & Pond spinet (light brown, driftwood color), Pitch Raise and Tuning done. A number of strings had broken as they were rusted and it was on average of 142 cents flat. Cable 402514, Tuning done. Hailun 198 313769 Tuning done, no issues found. Hailun 178 313793, Tuning done, no issues found. Young Chang 107 43936, Tuning done. Howard 316299, Tuning done. Kohler & Campbell 406261, Tuning done. Hailun 161 258653, Tuning done. Hailun 178 287342: install all metal hardware, metal hardware installed, need to cut down a continuous hinge in Nickel, and install it. Hallet Davis QRS 148 2788020, Tuning done. HD 148 2707439, Tuning done. Hailun 178 313357, Tuning done. Strich & Zeidler 4261, Tuning done. Hailun 121 277640, Tuning done, had to replace C6 jack as the jack toe had broken, repinned and replaced and regulated. Hailun HU-1P 289475, Tuning done. Chickering Grand in spot 60 was cleaned and polished with Cory High Gloss, as well as bright boy for the metal sections. Covered and ready for Saturday delivery. Harrington: 108453 I got started on this one on Friday, and removed the tuning pin bushings, and removed and had blown out the action stack, and used tsp on the key bed, and the sound board, need to remove the lid this next week to have better access to the back of the piano and more throughly clean the plate and sound board, and duplicate felts and remove the plate bolts and screws to polish to a mirror finish. Ran to Costco for Karmel to pick up some mints for the group, and I had found that Adeline had gotten some Baking Soda last week for the refinishing shop (we've got enough back there to last another month and a half according to Doris.)

Monday 01/03/25. KIMBALL 316512. Removed all keys from piano, installed them on board and apply baking soda, then numbered all keys. Removed all old key tops and removed old bushings.
Tuesday 01/04/25. Installed new bushings and put in new key tops up to key 61.
Wednesday 01/05/25. Finished installing new key tops, then removed old sharps and put in new ones.
Thursday 01/06/25. Router all new key tops through 3 and 4, then applied filler to damaged sharps. Finished project KIMBALL 316512
Friday 01/07/25. 
KRANICH&BACH 37121. Removed all keys from the piano, installed  them on the board and applied baking soda, then numbered them. Carefully removed all ivory key tops and then removed the old bushing.

Shattuck 16460: QC complete. I fixed the jumping chain - for real. Autopiano 185472: I've had this player in action only to discover choppy wind motor. I'm most the way through reupholstering the motor. I am very confident this player is close. Maybe another day. Tunings 1/2

Sara Sandberg: had a couple notes that needed some repinning in the action. Debbie Cram: there was just one note that needed to be raised a half step and corrected some unison tuning. Her strings are in pretty bad shape (corrosion). Schafer and Sons: Complete Kohler & Campbell 136433: had a lot of sticking keys due to some stiff sticker flanges. The stickers were very squeaky so I repinned some and used lubricant on several. Kohler & Campbell 406261: complete. Hailun HU-1P 289475: complete. Hailun 178 287342: did not get to this but it looks like the pedal rods are still getting replated. PRSB list: did not get to this. 8/10 tunings rescheduled.

Hailun 258357: yes, pulling the action out it clicked into place. Also, the right side brass pin into which the fall board rests was loose and wiggly causing 5 keys in the treble to be pressed down. I strategically placed a shim to lift the pin up, and it worked much better than I hoped for.  Hallet Davis 2788020: moved the damper guide rail as much as I could, but then shifted the dampers over one by one, Baldwin 66020: did exactly what Victoria did, which was to lower the let off buttons and increase drop, and that took care of it, Completed both the Mah Hailun 245397 and Hailun 1P 289475 out of order on the list, because Victoria was gone and I didn't need to consult her on these, Now I'm on the Walnut Hailun 316140: the first thing was that there was so little aftertouch I was instructed on this by setting let off and using a gauge to adjust the hammer height on some samples, (this was very good for me, because I needed to get some experience with the grand action), did 4 tunings this week,
Completed all voicing (5 pianos). Kimball 316512: Leveled keys, regulating in progress - doing after touch on white keys. 0/1 rescheduled, but she wants to upgrade her piano - (Adella Pearson)

Still need to reinstall the threshold piece in the vestibule, and organize the cables, and figure out the Ethernet situation in the vestibule.

Monday 10/02/25. KRANICH & BACH 37121. Puted in all new bushings and router 2 all key heads, then installed key tops up to key 61.
Tuesday 11/02/25. Finished putting in all top keys and then routered them through 3 and 4.
Wednesday 12/02/25. Removed all old sharps and installed all new ones. Finished project KRANICH & BACH 37121. LUDWING 79272. Put keys on board and applied baking soda, then numbered all keys.
Thursday 13/02/25. Removed all old bushings and removed all old ivory key tops, then installed new bushings up to 73.
Friday 14/02/25. Finished installing the new bushings and router 2 on all key fronts then installed the new keytops and passed them through router 3 and 4 up to key 16.

Autopiano 185472: Rebuilt wind motor and tracker. Finalized rebuild. I don't think it needs anymore QC than pre delivery. And you are privy to the awesomeness that this piano has become. Whitney 77521: initial assessment is that this player is in good shape, meaning I think it will be a smooth project. Have the upper body of player 3/4 shined up. Tunings 1/2

Schaeffer & sons 6'9": sustained bass notes were due to the fallboard being installed incorrectly. Baldwin 9' concert grand 66020: fixed. Hailun 178 287342: QC done. Yamaha C7 2750092: still not quite sure why it’s so noisy. Maybe because of its age? Hallet Davis 188 2788360: pedal squeak fixed. Did not get to these: Gulbransen 170677 (but I did install the pounder Monday night), Baldwin Acrosonic 402277, Wurlitzer 1293074. 7/8 tunings rescheduled.

Did 8 tunings this week (and it was awesome!) Walnut Hailun 245397: aligned hammers to strings, fixed sticking keys by easing them ever so gently, also I mentioned noticing the nearly non-existent aftertouch, which I addressed with raising hammer line, the let-off was pretty far from perfect, but I used the key off jig to adjust them, then quickly adjusted drop, Hailun 313793: spaced/aligned/squared hammers, aftertouch was easy, because let off was good, and raising the hammers did the job, tightened the lock nuts on the lyre supports,

Kimball 316512: Completed regulation, tuned, voiced, fixed bottom board, installed pedals, and regulated pedal. **there is a pedal rod missing - for the left two pedals (shared). Should we make a new pedal rod? Yamaha 1447632: Aligned and squared hammers. Reshaping half done.

Monday 02/17/25. LUDWING 79272. Sanded all sharps and delivered to paint shop, finished this project. 
Tuesday 02/18/25. KIMBALL 531140. Disassembled keyboard that was in the sales room, placed keys on board and applied baking soda, then numbered all keys. Removed old key tops and removed all bushings up to keys 63. 
Wednesday 02/19/25. Finished removing all bushings and installed new ones. 
Thursday 02/20/25. Routered all front keys, then installed all new key tops. 
Friday 02/21/25. Routered all new key tops 3 and 4, then cleaned and polished all sharps. Finished this project. 
GRINNELL 54523. I disassembled the keyboard, puted the keys on the board and applied baking soda, then numbered all the keys. Removed the old key tops.

Whitney 77521: have completed the upper body of player, including wind motor. Now have one of several auxiliaries rebuilt. Maybe a day or two on auxiliaries and several weeks for the striker body. Note: Ben is manufacturing the gear housing that fell apart. Also, if it's possible to have Jake do DHRT it's beneficial. Tunings 1/1

Matt Peterson: action just shifted and was propped up on something at the back. Becky Bullock: just had to add a leather bushing between the rod and the pedal. Cable 402514 (254): Complete. Baldwin 9' concert grand 66020 (143): Complete. Gulbransen 170677 (189a): In the middle of this one. Would need less than a day to finish. I believe the pedals are in plating. Baldwin Acrosonic 402277 (157): fixed sticking keys and about ready to finishing drilling holes for the pedal re-alignment. Should have that done in about 20 minutes. Wurlitzer 1293074 (82a): did not get to this one. I didn’t get to the PRSB list. 7/7 tunings rescheduled.

7 tunings this week, Walnut Hailun 121 278040: reduced blow distance and took up the lost motion, some let off adjustments and that seemed to fix aftertouch across the board, also tweaked some of backchecks,  Hailun 1P 251000: addressed aftertouch at the rest rail and picked up the lost motion slack; however several keys needed some punchings, some sections of the keys needed squaring/spacing, backchecks were way off (both in distance from string and centered with the catcher), (I tried not to take too long on this), lastly several hammers needed re-aligning/re-spacing, also I don't know all the steps for a New Piano Prep,

Yamaha 1447632: Finished reshaping hammers. leveled keys. set samples. regulated capstans. let off in progress.  Completed voicing on HD 1554743 (143), Irmler 10471 (104), and Brewster 63496. I went quick on Brewster 63496, might use a little more. I didn't finish voicing on Cable Nelson 186573 (43). I left it half done for the tour so they could hear the difference and forgot to finish it! The tour went really well. Running rescheduled 1/1

Just need the pedals which I didn’t ask Ben about on Friday. Gulbransen 170677 (189a): same as Foster. I read on Nacho’s past Friday report that he’d give. Hailun 121  322866: repaired jack toe, decreased the blow distance a bit, and tightened up the lost motion, Walnut Hallet Davis 2708056: did all the items on the showroom list, regulation needed a lot done and took some time, let off was way off, hammers needed a bunch of care,  Satin Hailun 121  243492: set rest rail to accommodate aftertouch mostly across the board. but had to remove some punchings on many of them, several of the keys we're misaligned, same for back checks, I gave the hammers a quick once over and made sure back checks are checking correctly, then of the 7 tunings this week 5 of them were schools, so final score is 1 out of 7, Hailun 151 287446: only just started on this one.

worked together for the QCs. I worked on Brewster 63496. Yamaha 1447632: completed Hallet Davis 31849: Got the capstans moved and everything put back together on the action. Still need to regulate samples to test. Smith & Nixon 20757: haven't started yet.

(Regulation needed on this piano, some keys have no after touch, and C8 needs more work as it rubs against B7 and sticks when played, possibly needs the center rail pin moved to the right, or the key needs to be further sanded down to fit, and I'm not so sure it's a bushing issue, but it could be. Hailun 151  287446: slowly working my way through the new piano prep, switched to Mah Hailun 5P 282881 when Victoria wasn't available, it required a lot of adjustment but I'm done with the new piano prep on this one, went back to a customer to repair the jack on a spinnet with drop action, had to take the action out but it went really well, tuning score was 1 out of 4, but 2 of those were at Peteetneet Museum and they call us regularly,

Hallet Davis 31849: completed regulation - didn't find cabinetry, need to get cheekblocks and fall board from Julio. Gulbransen 191466: completed voicing. Smith & Nixon 20757: Removed bass bridge cap, inserted shoe pegs.

Tuning=0 out of 2, Astin Weight 15021: got a jump start on the refurb by applying the 60/40 solution a few times to hammer butt flanges…. Hailun 121 #303784: was able to do new piano prep reasonably quickly,  Louis Zwickie 10149: cleaned up hammers, adjust some let off, I didn't date decreasing blow distance (less than 1 1/2" already) so I subtracted 0.25" from all the punchings across the board, and that solved the dip & aftertouch, there was clicking at F#3 and G#3, but I couldn't figure out why, so I just left it, Baldwin Acrosonic 938810: picking up from where Stephen left off (it looks like he was about to level the keys) did that and set rest rail as close as I dared, then increased aftertouch with dip by subtracting punchings (I tried to move along fast by going for "A" work only, straightened the worst hammers, Brite Boy on visible brass, and reassembled cabinetry, it needed a deep cleaning, cleaned back of piano, made it look nice, Friday was a half-day, and I'm just getting back to the Astin Weight,
Hailun 121 303784: completed qc. Walnut Hailun 151 316140: completed qc. Curtis H took the music desk to fix a spot. Bernareggi 19507: Completed cleaning. started action prep Friday.

Tuning 0 out of 2; however, with both customers I spoke at length about a tuning in 6 months, Yamaha U10A #4797617: I picked up with the refurb with tightening action screws/lubrication, aligned/spaced hammers, settled on a blow distance that (after let off & lost motion) ended up with good aftertouch throughout, back checks then reassembled cabinetry & some light cleaning, also Brite Boy on BB pedals & brass & YAMAHA letters (this took only 13 minutes), Astin Weight 12051: after several applications the alcohol/water solution didn't work for 49 of the hammer butt flanges, did a deep cleaning of piano, Brite Boy on visible brass while waiting for solution to dry, I'm repinning the flanges now, Steinway 355128 (actual serial number must be 355128): cleaned plate and soundboard, but the plate screws weren't ready, so I went back to the Astin Weight, due to some doctors appts. and a tuning took 2 hours and 2 hours back reduced the amount I wanted to achieve this week,

Yamaha 800156: completed voicing. Hailun HU-5P 130304: completed qc. Astin Weight 12051: fixed ringing damper and general damper regulation. gave a pitch raise and voiced (still needs final tuning). I don't know where the work is at on this one, but it also needs hammer alignment and tighten hammer flanges. It also has some slow hammer returns, maybe pinning. Bernareggi 19507: Just about finished with all cleaning action prep. Will finish in about 30 min.

Monday, May 19, 2025. Placed all keycaps from previous projects in the CNC room in chronological order, a total of 15 sets. WEBER 43336. Finished installing all new keycaps, sanded all blanks, and deliver them to the paint shop.
Tuesday, May 20, 2025. Carefully cleaned all ivory keycaps and carefully sanded all painted blanks to a satin finish. Finalize project WEBER 43336. WALWORTH 30357. Placed the keycaps on the keycap board and apply baking soda. Numbered them and removed all old keycaps.
Wednesday, May 21, 2025. Installed all new keycaps and removed all old blanks. Thursday, May 22, 2025. Installed all new plastic caps and completed project WALWORTH 30357. CUNNINGHAM 16124. Removed all piano keys and placed them on the keyboard; then apply baking soda and numbered them.
Friday, May 23, 2025. Removed  all old caps and installed new ones up to key 70.

Autopiano 99849:striker and double valve body broken down. All gaskets need replacing and most are done. The valve leather was in good condition. Connor told me to leave the strikers as is. (Based on pliability) so I'm in the middle of going through one by one checking existing. If there aren't many problems we just saved a week or two.  I know the pianos regulation and trap need work.

Matthew Healey: picked up bench and fixed a tri-chord damper that wasn’t seated all the way. Hallet Davis 2788020: damper guide rail adjustment complete. Andrew Kohler 78279 (49): complete. Marshall & Wendell 75866 (83): just need one more day on this and then I’ll be ready for another project. Tunings 8/8 plus 1 additional tuning that was a no-show.

Tuning 0 out of 2, Astin Weight 12051: Completed refurb except for the remaining sluggish hammers and a few dampers which have stumped me, I'll need some direction with repinning the hammer flanges (the hammers with blue masking tape are the ones which I had already repinned), Steinway 355193 (I mistakenly thought the serial # was 355128): I'm in the restringing now, there was some mix up with the action bolts, but I was able to figure out the correct order, one of the capo bar screws broke, but I found a match in salvage and had Korban sand/buff it, I don't think  I'll be in for Memorial Day

Yamaha U1 #2757140 completed QC. Bernareggi 19507: completed CAP. Astin Weight 12051: completed QC. In addition to slow hammers, there were a lot of slow jacks with tight pinning causing repetition issues. I repinned problematic hammers and lubricated jacks with Protek CLP. Everett 34956: completed CAP. Friedich Prein 120271: cleaning in progress. Disassembled. Keybed finished. Trapwork finished. Working on casters.

Removed the piano keys, placed them on the board, applied baking soda, and numbered them. Then removed all the old bushings.
Friday, May 30, 2025. Installed all the new bushings, then carefully cleaned all the sharps. Completed project STEINWAY D 423387

Autopiano 99849: upper main player body restored and tested. All strikers nominal. Time to move player into piano. Whitney 77521: piano and player action good. Added a few felts. Looked into the askew front panel. That board is warped. There's nothing we can do short of making a new front panel. Other than a few missing labels for the pallet buttons (bass,treble,sus. )  the piano is ready. I will say that the reroll on this one is funky but I think it's due to the homemade bracket.

Sherry DuMoulin: found a pill bottle behind the piano that was touching the soundboard. Alice Walker: bench delivered. Kara Healey: bench dropped off. Kimball 176797: voicing complete. Young Chang 43936: QC started by me and finished up by Adaline. Marshall & Wendell 75866: PRSB complete. Steinway 38930: measuring strings now. Will need all next week to continue this. Tunings 7/8 rescheduled.

Worked 4 days, Tuning 1 out of 2, Steinway 355193: really wanted to finish up this week (still waiting for the bass strings), but a lot of time was consumed by getting out a broken tuning pin, but I've got a good rhythm now,

Acrosonic 343593: completed QC. Friedich Prein 120271: Completed cleaning. Got the sides and lid off! The lid got some cracks in the finish in the process of taking it off. Both of the sides have cracks in the wood. Is it better for me to repair those cracks in the sides now? -- Action prep in process. Started Friday morning. Tuning reschedule 1/1.

Whitney 77521: finalized piano for delivery. Autopiano 99849: buffing, leathers and felts for linkage. There's quite bit of rust removal to be done for under key bed. I think I'm several days more for reinsertion of parts and then testing in piano. I have a very good feeling about this player not being weak or finicky.

Peteetneet Museum warranty: just had to shim the key slip. Steinway 38930: will finish the cleaning in about one more day and then will need the rest of the week for action prep. 5/5 Tunings rescheduled.

Tuning=0 out of 1,Hailun 121 #326318: did the QC and filled out the checklist, also when playing the pianos in the showroom I fixed a grand on which the soft pedal caused some keys to strike 2 notes, also Baldwin Acrosonic #938810: it was moved to showroom (117) directly from refinishing and I had to install pedals and regulate them; however, I noticed that note F2 is stuck so bad that the key remains pressed down, please advise on how to fix it, Steinway 355193: was almost finished restringing once the bass strings got here; however, I got to a point that they mixed up the sequence, and so I'm carefully working from the first string up to find the mix up,Yamaha U3 #1630946: fixed pedals that were scraping against their slots and added some felts to reduce the clunking, tightened all the loose plate screws I could reach, vacuumed up some evidence of mice, installed rubber bumper buttons,  Hailun 121 #309130: tightened some very loose action screws, chose a felt size for rest rail resulting in plenty of aftertouch to spare, adjusted pedals, Weber 118620: reshaped hammers, bolstered knuckles, tightened screws, lubricated, traveled hammers, I'll align them to strings and begin regulation on Monday, Also, next week probably don't schedule me on Thursday June 12, because I have a colonoscopy and don't know when the anesthesia will wear off,

Baldwin SF-10 370192: completed bridge repair. Cleaned up and put back together.  Brought the treble strings up to pitch, but did not otherwise tune. Ludwig 79272: Completed voicing. Friedich Prein 120271: glued cracks on sides. Action prep in progress. We had to order hammer butts to match, and will probably use original flanges. Will probably finish the rest of the action Monday and see if the hammer butts are in by then.

YAMAHA P2 800156. I removed the old keys and installed the new ones up to key 51.

Autopiano 99849: happy to report in this week I was able to finalize all the small details ending in an incredible instrument.  She's fine tuned, tuned, and thoroughly tested. By far the finest and most responsive player I have ever played. Quite a juxtaposition from the Whitney. There is a rod for a mandolin bar but I haven't seen the bar if there was one. Excited to start the Knabe! Schedule a time for us to meet. Tuning 0/1

Steinway 38930: cleaning finished. I noticed the hammer shanks and whippens have already been replaced. There was also a box of used hammers that was sitting by the action. The hammers currently on the shanks look pretty new. Are we doing anything with the hammers? If not, I'll finish action prep on Monday. 6/6 tunings rescheduled.

Steinway 355193: finished restringing save for that one incorrect string (for which Victoria ordered a new one),  Samick 8510474: installed lyre & regulated pedals,  Weber 118620: finished regulation, repaired a part on the lyre, lubricated & regulated pedals, Brite Boyed all the visible brass, Baldwin SF-10 #370192: thorough cleaning, repaired lyre, adjusted the cheek blocks so they hold the key frame straight and even, Brite Boyed all visible brass, now I'm reshaping the hammers,

Friedich Prein 120271: completed action prep. Christman 15400: completed QC. Steinway 113162: completed qc. Yamaha U171459: completed QC. Mathushek 8409: started Thursday. ribs and perimeter are good. Cut shim trenches, out of shims, so started bridges.

Janssen 123519: legs glued and screwed and plugged. They are waiting by Julio's drying rack. Knabe 116388: lid, lyre, fall board, action, and player drawer removed. 3/4 through hose replacement on drawer. It's hard to estimate how long it will take to get those auxiliaries pulled and serviced. We're in this one for a minute. Who's going to rebuild the Knabe? No tunings.

Amy Payne: the A0 hammer was catching on the damper guide rail causing it not to return. I moved the guide rail but also shaved off a sliver of the wood from the underside. Had squeaky QRS pedal components as well which she didn't mention but I lubricated it. Steinway 38930: just need about 30 minutes to clean up some epoxy and I'll be done with PRSB. Will maybe need a day to finish with action prep so I'm about ready for a new project. 6/6 tunings rescheduled.

Short week- worked only MON  TUE  WED, Steinway 355193: put on the last bass string as soon as it came in and handed it off for DHRT, Baldwin SF-10  370192, plowed thru the tefurb, bolstered/lubed knuckles, reshaped/travelled/aligned hammers (only a handful of the flanges needed repinning), burned a few shanks, cleaned/straightened/leveled keys (less than a dozen needed punchings), now trying to pace myself thru regulation,
Curtis at YM camp

The only thing left on this aspect is rerouting of 12 hoses somewhere down the line but I have them labeled for identification. This coming week I will start hose replacement for aux. under S. B.  So I don't lose any more routing by accident. Once that is complete I will drop the motor and tip the piano on its side.    -   Connor and I both agree to smash out the Gulbranson first. I intend to devote several hours a day until complete.

Dwayne & Tami King: had to repin several hammer flanges. Steinway 38930: complete. Chickering 8’: cleaning in progress. I removed all the plate screws and bolts but it looks like the plate is built into the casing on all sides. Not sure how to remove it. Will need next week to continue this. I’ll be taking the 4th of July off as well. 5/6 tunings rescheduled.

Tuning = 0 for 1, Baldwin SF-10  370192: circled thru regulation process, and finished refurb, please look at the ding on the left side of the spanner right near the rubber button, I was putting the action in and lost balance and I bumped it there, and I don't know if the ding there is from me, also I noticed some scratches there, too. Weber 43336: working thru the teardown and cleaning, I miraculously got all the grime off the back of piano (I'm super proud of how this turned out), I'm now making repairs on the trapwork,

Weber G118620: completed voicing  Baldwin 41428: completed QC and items on list. Andrew Kohler 78279: completed voicing. Mathushek 8409 (50): paused soundboard work to prioritize Prein. shims are finished. Bridges in progress. Prein 120271 (67): perimeter and ribs almost finished - just need to plug some screw holes. Shims are next.

Everett 36787: Done! Wurlitzer 983993: Restringing in progress. I will need about another two days to finish. I will be going to the PTG convention in a couple weeks so I will need to take July 15th to 18th off.

Baldwin 41428, Tuning done, and cleaned for delivery. Everett 179572, finished soda blasting the piano, and I applied Fire Clean to every surface of the piano. I replaced the batteries for the alarm system near the entrance to the middle shop from the parking lot, however it's still not getting power to it, and I'm not exactly certain as to why that is. I'm hoping I might somehow be able to study it up on my off time, and get it figured out. Possibly an electrical issue, other than the batteries? Steinway 220857, Tuesday, Tuning done. Wellington 155111, Tuning done. Coinola 75443, Tuning done. Vacuum filters cleaned and buckets emptied. Benches near spot 61 have been assembled and placed before pianos that lack benches. Ludwig 79272, this pianos cabinetry and trap work has been completely reassembled. Friedrich Prein I buffed and polished both (I think they're Capo Bar for this piano? Curtis would know.) I'm working on the screws as well as the agraffs.

Monday, June 30, 2025. ANDREW KOHLER 78278. Removed all the old key tops and installed the new ones up to key 59.
Tuesday, July 1, 2025. Finished installing the new key tops, then routed them through routers 3 and 4. Finished project ANDREW KOHLER 78279.
Wednesday, July 2, 2025. Everett 179572. Removed the old bushings and installed the new ones up to key 34.
Thursday, July 3, 2025. Finished installing the new bushings, then polished all the keys. Finished project Everett 179572.
Friday, July 4, 2025. US Holiday

Gulbranson 170677: clean and organize nachos space and projects. Re-prepped strikers and decks up to cutting of new material and gluing hinges on strikers. It was found that the ports in the striker rails were seeping air out the pores of the wood. Had to back pressure and force shellac into those pores. Connor will take it from here for now. Knabe 116388: Had a few more aux. to restore in the drawer. Drawer is now fully ready and rebuilt. No tunings.

Marilyn Christensen: just had a tight bushing on a spinet action sticker. Janssen 123519: complete. Wick 145540: complete. Steinway 220857: QC complete. Baldwin 370193: voicing complete. Wellington 155111: still need about three hours on this QC. Chickering 8': moving into action prep so I'll need the week to finish that.  1/4 tunings personally rescheduled. One of those tunings already has future tunings scheduled with us.

Weber 43336: glued cracks, replaced felts & leathers on trap work, got the key bed off, measured and removed bass strings and treble strings, took out tuning pins, made detailed map of plate screws and removed them, however I'm stumped as to how to remove the action bolts, because there is nowhere to grab to unscrew them,

Kimball B72797: completed voicing and QC. Everett 36737: completed voicing. Bush & Lane 17827: completed voicing and QC. Coinola 175443: completed QC and fixed middle pedal. Completed Jake Hansen issues. Chickering 80147: QC in progress. Needs a little more time. --Didn't make it to working on the Prein this week. Some of the QC's took more time.

Wurlitzer 983993: Restringing is done! I will start regulating on Monday and get as much done before I leave for the convention.

Kimball B72797, Tuning done. Bush & Lane 17827 Tuning done. Definitely will need a full a through regulation of the action. A5 was fixed as it had a lot of lost motion, however the capstan was adjusted pretty high in relation to the other keys. Everett 36737, Tuning done. Chickering 80147, Tuning done, A6, and A#6 are moving with their neighbors, and D5 is rubbing against the key slip, needs regulated. Hailun 121 156973, Tuning done. Tuning pins bass side of Bichord on B3, and C4 were loose, tap with a hammer? Clicking was found on F#5, as well as bass notes from A0-C#2. Hailun 5P 282881, Tuning done. Ludwig 79272, Tuning done. Hailun 121 309130, Tuning done. Promberger 2263, Tuning done. A4, and A#4 will need to be tuned better, I tired with what I had, but it wasn't fitting on the pins near the plate. I'll get to that first thing next week.  Weber G118620, Tuning done. nickel trim HD 148 2707439, Tuning done. Hailun 178 313357, Tuning done. Wick 14540: used TSP & Alcohol to try and clean up the areas that were tested. I think it may have worked? Vacuum filters were cleaned out. Everett 36737: Casters, Pedals, pedal trim, and hinges were polished with a mix of metal wax and bright boy. Friedrich Prein: 120271, buffed the plate bolts and screws. I'm having a hard time removing the tarnished bits from the mounting bolts for the plate, maybe I'll sand them a bit on the sides and edges and see if that works? I also put some bolts in the tumbler with the metal magic to further clean and polish them! This project should be done Monday. Chickering Phan: I started Soda Blasting the action and key frame, but feel as though this one is a little tougher than usual, plus the soda blasting guns tend to be a bit sensitive as to their effectiveness.

Monday, July 7, 2025. MASON & HAMLIN 80682. All old key caps were removed and new ones installed up to key 28.
Tuesday, July 8, 2025. The new key caps were installed and then routed using routers 3 and 4. Project MASON & HAMLIN 80682 was completed.
Wednesday, July 9, 2025. STORY AND CLARK 900975. All old key caps were removed and new ones installed up to key 35.
THURSDAY, July 10, 2025. All new key caps were installed and then routed using routers 3 and 4. Project STORY AND CLARK 900975 was completed.
Friday, July 12, 2025. CUNNINGHAM 16124. Removed old key caps and fitted new ones up to key 39.
Knabe 116388: pulled wind motor. Very comprehensive on rebuild. Has to be solid. Need another half day to get that rapped up and move to the next Aux. no tunings.

Wellington 155111: QC Complete. Ludwig 79272: QC Complete. Hailun 5P 282881: QC Complete. Chickering 8’: Currently on action prep. The action and key bed is in the table saw room waiting to be soda blasted. I’ll need all next week to finish this. I have a question about what exactly needs to be replaced on the action since it’s not the traditional hammer/whippen. 5/6 tunings rescheduled.

Tuning 1 out of 4, but one was a church and another the contact person had left, also I was caught in traffic on the way back from 3 of them,  QC on Hailun 121 156973, Ludwig 79272 thoroughly cleaned back of piano & gave the rest of cabinetry a quick going over, Weber 43336: can't remember everything, cleaned key bed and Scotch Brited front & balance rail pins, new bushings for the guides for the pedal rods, then sanded the rods so they glide smoothly, doing action prep now,

Chickering 80147: completed voicing and QC. Aeolian 96128: completed voicing. Nordiska Grand DG00831: completed voicing.  Prein 120271: Completed soundboard repairs. I'm at a point that I'll need the bridge very soon to continue. Mathushek 8409: Prepping for bridges.

Tuesday, July 15, 2025. I finished routing all the new key tops through routers 3 and 4. I finished all the keys. I began disassembling the piano according to Evan's instructions.
