Thursday, July 17, 2025. I removed the casters and installed the new ones. I measured all the strings and filled out the form.
Friday, July 18, 2025. I continued disassembling, measured the string thickness, and began removing them.

Knabe 116388: completed and reinstalled wind motor , electric motor, ,several auxiliaries, and have all the major hosing replaced minus the stack. I tested the wind motor/electric motor up to the pressure regulator. Great suction and steady regulator. Next I have a few more auxiliaries to do before I drop the stack (one piece). The stack will be several weeks on restore. Also helped garret fix and coat the pin block veneer. Tuning 0/1

Gulbransen 317767: Voicing done. Kurtzmann 95827: QC done. Fallboard does rub at the top when almost fully closed. It also needs voiced.Joseph Sorensen: bobbing hammer and sluggish hammers fixed. Anna White: buzzing fixed. Yamaha 818851: Refurb started today. Will need next week to finish this. Chickering 8': key bed now soda blasted and I assume the action is next. 4/5 tunings rescheduled.

Tuning = 2 out of 4, Weber 43336: lifted out plate, cleaned soundboard, carefully numbered and band sawed the hammer heads and placed them in storage, steamed off dampers, blasted/cleaned/new materials on Rest rail, spring rail, Let Off,installed new hammer butts, tightened & lubricated action, some minor repairs on sostenuto mechanism (not fully knowing how it all worked), the only thing left to do for CAP is to re-glue all the jack flanges,

Kurtzmann 95827: completed voicing. Autopiano 99849: completed QC. Apollo 123770: completed voicing. Bechstein 88735: Installed lyre and brought pitch up. Started regulation. Shouldn't need too much more - a day of regulation and cleaning it out, then tuning, and it should be good to go.

Monday, July 21, 2025. CUNNINGHAM 16124. I finished removing the strings and then removed all the screws from the plate.
Tuesday, July 22, 2025. The plate was removed and the rest of the piano carefully cleaned. Then the pedals were disassembled and all the felts replaced.
Wednesday, July 23, 2025. All the felts were removed from the keybed and new felts were applied, then all the pins were sanded.
Thursday, July 24, 2025. Durfey family keyboard. I removed all the old keycaps and replaced the new ones.
Friday, July 25, 2025. I removed all the old sharps and replaced the new ones up to key 36. I routered 3 and 4 of the new keycaps, up to key 35.

Knabe 116388: finished 3 more auxiliaries to complete all aux. dropped the stack and mapped hosing. Some dismantling of stack. Gulbranson: spent 2 more days rebuilding the trunk and redesigning hose connections.  Tunings 1/2

Steinway 356752: fixed unresponsive A0 (missing whippen) and fixed the broken string. Had some damper issues but that’s it and everything else worked fine. Michael Hansen Warranty: fixed a sluggish hammer and sustain issue. Durfeys Key: picked up keys and key frame and told them it would be about 2-3 weeks. Bubinga Irmler 10471: QC complete. Several key tops had come unglued causing clicking noises. Bubinga Pramberger J00020603: almost done. I found that the music desk is off centered so it rubs at the bottom when adjusting it up and down. Just maybe 30 minutes to finish the QC. Doug Hymas: installed the muffler rail which was fun to do. He seemed happy with it. Yamaha 818851: basically done with the cleaning and will start on the action next. Will need a week to finish that. Chickering 8’ grand: can start fixing soundboard cracks/rib separation next week as well. Will need the week to work on that. I have a check up with my doctor on Monday at 3 which shouldn’t take too long. I put it on my calendar. 7/8 tunings rescheduled. The 8th tuning was a school.

Tuning =2 out of 3, warranty repair on the Nordisk in Ogden: a rep lever spring came out of its slot causing note not to play, while there I spot-voiced the worst offenders that sounded overly bright when delivered to spacious living room with high ceiling & wood floor, Weber 43336: finished re-glueing jack flanges, and made sure the whole sostenuto rail could easily slide up and down; however, I was unsure how to make each key work, Everett 179572: really good cleaning, aligned/spaced/reshaped hammers,lubricated & tightened action screws (every one of which needed it badly)…as well as the plate screws (which I got as many as I could reach), I forgot to tighten the capo bar screws before Korban tuned it, but now I'm not going to touch it,Monday morning I'll level keys & start regulation, I work only Monday & Tuesday next week,

Wurlitzer 983993: completed QC. put tape on for Julio to look at. Couldn't find music desk. Maybe Julio knows where it is? Steinway 355193: Completed voicing. Bechstein 88735: Completed gut work. Left cleaning cabinetry until after repairs. Kimball 176797: Completed QC. Starck 74040: Started cleaning, then switched to Wellington. Wellington 155111: Took apart, put on back, and ready to start bass bridge.

Sherman Clay G015099: A6 and brass lid alignment complete. Yamaha 818851: currently repinning and bosltering hammers. will need about four more days to finish this project. 8/10 Tunings Rescheduled.

Tuning= 2 out of 4, (but the other 2 are a school), Hailun #239862, new prep, left let-off where it was and adjusted blow distance and lost motion to get a universal aftertouch, fixed a clicking sound, Yamaha U1 #4465930: an abbreviated QC, touched up let off, and that brought the aftertouch in line across the board, made backchecks check closer, Everett 179572: finished up refurb and put everything back together, no nicks or scratches in the finish, Astin Weight 8017: started refurb, pedal rods were missing, but I found rods in salvage which I cut to length and stuck bridge pins in the ends, turned out perfect, did a warranty repair in Mapleton,

Everett 34956: completed voicing. Taylor 23325: completed bass termination installation and QC. Wellington 155111: completed QC. Sherman Clay G015099: completed QC. Starck 74040 (258): completed refurb, made lyre supports, did a pin block treatment and tuned, voiced, QC for showroom complete. Knabe 84574 (64): Started Friday afternoon. It needs a good amount of regulation work. --- Prein: did you do the apron with the CNC machine or just the bridge? I had given you the apron as well.

Chickering 8' prepping to fix some perimeter separation along the front of the soundboard. Fixed one spot underneath. Doesn't appear to be any more perimeter separation. Will need the week to continue this project. 5/7 tunings scheduled a year out.

Curtis in Argentina

Thursday, August 21, 2025.
I went through routers 3 and 4 and finished the project. EVERETT 36737. BINKERHOFF 181168. Removed the piano keys, then removed the old ivory key tops, recovering 50% of the keys. Then I pressed the new key tops up to key 66.
Friday, 08/22/2025. I finished installing all the new key tops and routed them through routers 3 and 4 up to key 78.

Gulbranson 170677: been doing various tweeks to get this player playing. Now I have the main body and all strikers working correctly but something is off with how the trunk is transferring air to the wind motor depending if ON or OFF. I will have to pull the trunk again and identify the issue. Once that is done I think we're close. Tunings 0/1

Kurtzmann 95827: QC complete. Kawai KG-2C 980254: QC complete. Everett 36737: Need one more day on this QC. I noticed several of the tabs on the dampers dont work properly so the sostenuto fuction wont work 100%. Once the tab is pushed down it stays down and wont get caught by the blade when it needs to. Chickering 8' Grand: I believe I got all the perimeter and rib separation and I've started on the sound board shimming. Will need another week to get this done. 7/8 tunings rescheduled. The 8th was the Slate Canyon Youth Center. I also have a Huntsman Cancer Institute event in Salt Lake this coming tuesday and would need to get off at 3pm to get there in time.

Tuning 0 for 4, but the primary contact on 2 wasn't there, and one was a High School, Astin Weight 8017: I had really hoped to be done with it, but kept needing repairs, bridle straps so brittle that even gentle handling tore them, repinned some jack flanges, replaced jack springs, several dampers hanging by a thread, scraped off some candle wax drippings in action, and everything was more than twice as hard because of the design— the key bed angled downward made getting to everything hard, keys were difficult getting out, and several pulled out the center pins when taking the keys out, key leveling & let off and lost motion took forever, for Monday I've got to finish aftertouch, back checks and dampers still, one thing I realized on Friday was the piano was located in a dark corner, and the only available work tables were dark corner ones as well, first thing Monday will be to move the piano to where there's lots of light, that really makes it 3 times easier for me if there's lots of light, if only I hadn't tried to tough it out in the dark,  I bet I'd be done by now,  with this in mind some more hanging work lights would pay for themselves within a week, for Monday I need to finish aftertouch, backchecks, dampers, and replace missing caster,
Astin Weight 8017: completed voicing. Brinkerhoff 181163: completed voicing. Smith & Nixon 20757: completed voicing. Knabe 84574: regulation in progress. I think it will be ready in two more days.

Tuesday, August 26, 2025. Polished all the key tops and replaced the missing key heads.
Wednesday, August 27, 2025. Completed KEMBLE 80755. CUNNINGHAM 16124. Disassembled the action and applied  baking soda.
Thursday, August 28, 2025. Steam removed all the felts from the action.
Friday, August 29, 2025. Removed all the old hammers and continued working according to the instructions.
Gulbranson 170677: had been trying to figure out issues until we decided to install feeder bellows. Knabe 116388: started recovering strikers. 20 done.

Kurtzmann 95827: squeak fixed. Decker 4870: F#2 and G2 fixed. Everett 36737: QC done. Still has some sostenuto tabs that are stiff or aren’t working properly. Chickering 8’ grand: soundboard cracks underneath are complete. You can take a look and see if it’s good enough. So the piano can be tipped right side up again. I still have one shim to clean up and then I can check down bearing on the bridges. If the bridges are good, I’ll finish PRSB on Monday and will need a new project. 6/7 tunings rescheduled.

Tuning 1 out of 2, Astin Weight 8017: replaced casters, restringed treble note and measured bass strings (Evan ordered them),the final thing was damper timing, bass dampers were engaging so much earlier that I tried adjusting bass sustain pedal, and…. crack! The bass sustain pedal (the rocker arm) broke clean thru, (Thank you Brigham for making me a new one) but after installing it I discovered that the bass pedal rod broke right at the spot where it is activated by the sustain pedal rod, luckily I found a bass pedal rod in the salvaged actions, and with a few modifications it works fine, Knabe 128695: trying to rocket thru the teardown to make up for lost time, on Monday I'll be working from 1 - 3pm for a tuning in Orem,

Did the end of month piano updates. Completed 1 online piano appraisal. Knabe 84574: Almost done. Completed most of the regulation and voicing. Just need to regulate dampers, and then assemble and QC. Probably finish on Monday.

Monday, September 1 to Thursday, September 5, 2025. CUNNINGHAM 16124. I disassembled all mechanical components of the action and replaced the felts on each. I repaired several damaged parts, and there were 4 or 5 previously broken parts that cannot be repaired because they compromise the movement pins.
Friday, September 5, 2025. MASON AND HAMLING 26068. I removed the piano keys, then placed them on the board and applied baking soda. I numbered them. I removed all the old bushings and carefully removed all the ivory key tops up to key 25.

Knabe 116388: finished recovering strikers and rebuilding the wind chest. Unit is reinstalled in the piano with all Aux hoses connected. I did a quick test and I have at least seen a handful of strikers activate. With the way this system is designed I can't run a full test without all component vacuum connected. I also put power to the scroll motor and it works. Now I mainly have the 3 sets of 88 hoses to cut and connect for the drawer and reconfiguring the expression ends of the tracker bar. I intend to switch back to the Gulbranson Monday.  No tunings.

Kawai 690265 (252): sharps glued on.  Steinway 99151 (153): F6/F#6 fixed. Gebr Schulz 824003 (119): key slip shimmed. Steinway 443694 (142): voiced. Kranich & Bach 37121 (89): Strings installed. Birdseye Kimball 485236 (118): F#2 and one others fixed. Astin Weight 8017 (67): QC about halfway done. The piano is a bit wobbly and the last pair of bass strings is missing. Possibly being ordered? Steinway 355193 (44): QC complete. Wurlitzer 983993 (83a): QC complete. Chickering 8’: almost done restoring back action. Will need about half a day to finish. Did not get to the Herrburger yet. Tunings 5/5

Tunings = 2 out of 4, did a warranty repair, Knabe 12895: I can't remember the things I did, but I only need to remove agraffes & give them to Korban,make a map of the plate screws, remove them, take out plate and clean soundboard, and then I'll be done with the CAP,

Knabe 84574: all finished. Walworth 30357: Moved downstairs, installed plate - adjusted shims for down bearing, buffed screws that got missed, installed plate felt, prepped for stringing, ready to start actual stringing.

Monday, September 8, 2025. MASON & HAMLIN 26068. Finished removing all the ivory, recocerse most of it. Installed the new bushings up to key 55.
Tuesday, September 9, 2025. Finished installing all the new bushings, then installed the new key tops up to key 45.
Wednesday, September 10, 2025. Finished installing all the new key tops, then routered 3 and 4 through key 47.
Thursday, September 11, 2025. Finished routered 3 and 4 on all key tops, then carefully sanded all the sharp and painted them. Completed MASON & HAMLIN 26068 project.
Friday, September 12, 2025. BALDWIN 223532. Removed the keyboard from the piano and numbered the keys, removed all the old bushings and installed the new ones up to keyboard 39.

Knabe 116388: player and piano action installed. All hosing connected. As you know initial tests are very promising. Have some head scratching minutiae to sort out but I am confident this should hold as a fantastic restoration once finalized. Gulbransen 170677: started restore of feeder bellows. Maybe 2 more days on that. Tunings 1/2

Astin Weight 8017: QC done, assembled, and moved to showroom. Chickering 8': Complete. Elden Brooks: tuned the new string and put a felt in to mute it. One buzzing sound was glue on the damper and the main buzz coming from the fallboard went away with tightening screws. Herrburger Brooks 4219: Plate and screws installed. Felt made, starting to restring. Will need the week to complete this. Tunings: 4/5.

Tuning = 3 out of 5, I couldn't find the ringing sound on the low note(s) warranty repair we'll have to send Jake or Mckinly to figure it out (and also I had to really bang on some notes which might have thrown them out of tune but I ran out of time, Knabe 128695: finished CAP, Knabe 41783: working my way thru teardown, but switched to the action because it has a unique double flange for hammer & damper (We will need to 3D print some with all the holes in the right places, and a sample flange is on a table in the main shop - you can't miss it), soda blasted action, taped together the section ends hammers, replacing let off buttons,

Walworth 30357: drilling the holes wide, then filling with epoxy, then redrilling for new pins. Got a couple test pins finished and it worked great. Top section drying. Middle section is drilled out. -- Jimenez regulation was successful. It didn't actually need a lighter touch. It needed more aftertouch with the original dip so it was easier to play loud. The daughter likes playing big, and it was more tiring to try to do that with decreased dip.

Everett 37998, Removed all strings, strings and plate, New Casters leveled, Cleaned Sound board, Trapwork, and parts of Action, Currently working on the action.

Steinway 96464, Tuning done. Yamaha G2 769615, Tuning done. Samick 8510474, Tuning done. Hailun V126 V86850, Tuning done. Aeolian 96128, Tuning done. Steinway 121109, Soundboard, and Plate were cleaned. Hallet Davis 188 2788360, Tuning done. Chickering 80147, Tuning done. Hailun 198 313769, Tuning done. Kimball 316512, Tuning done. Knabe 128695: Buffing done. Began Buffing for Petrof 2046, at least I believe that's what it's called.

Monday, September 15, 2025. BALDWIN 223532. I finished installing the new bushings, then removed the old key tops and installed the new ones up to key 40.
Tuesday, September 16, 2025. I finished installing the new key tops and routed them through routers 3 and 4 up to key 51.
Wednesday, September 17, 2025. I finished project BALDWIN 223532. KNABE 41783. I removed the piano keys and placed them on the keyboard, then applied Bakin Soda and numbered them.
Thursday, September 18, 2025. I removed the old bushings and installed the new ones up to key 21.
Friday, September 19, 2025. I finished removing all the old bushings and installed the new ones. I removed all the old key tops and installed the new ones up to key 51.

Gulbransen 170677: feeders took a bit longer than expected but it is done and mounted in the piano now. Initial tests look good. Great vacuum. Friday I custom built a sustain pneumatic to fit.  Next I will custom make linkage and then try to get the gearing to not sound so rickety. We are actually getting close on this I believe. Tunings 1/2

Johannes Seiler Grand IKNFG0114: complete. Eldon Brooks: thought I got rid of the buzzing or at least calmed it down but he texted me on my way home for lunch that more buzzing was happening. I talked to Melissa about this and she was going to talk to you. Herrburger Brooks 4219: will finish restringing Monday and will move on to DHRT for the week. Would have finished Friday but I attended the vigil for Charlie Kirk at UVU with Ezzy. Tunings: 4/7

Tuning= 3 out of 5, did a warranty repair, Knabe 41783: had to repair some dampers & flanges before installing new hammer butts, however 7 double flanges were beyond repair, I'll need to get the 3D printed double flanges and bush them before I can do dampers/hammer butts #60 - #66, tightened screws, new let off buttons, polished damper lever rod, lubed action, new felts on spring rail & rest rail, I need to reglue jack flanges to be done with the action, then I'll finish taking off the strings & the plate,

Steinway 96464: completed voicing. Birdseye Kimball 485236: Completed voicing. Straightened F5 and a couple other keys. --Did an in home appraisal Friday afternoon. Walworth 30357: With the voicings and appraisal, only had 1 day of work on this. Filled more holes with epoxy. Drilled out the bass section. On Friday, drilled the dried epoxy for no. 2 pins. Drilled out the section that didn't dry.

Installed new dampers
Numbered new hammers
Cut all hammers shorter to match original short hammers

Baldwin 223532: Done! Young Chang 801927: In progress. I will need until about Thursday to continue and finish.

Everett 37998: Continued with Action Prep. Removed dampers, replaced Hammer butts, checked each flange for cracking noises. Replaced all the buttons on the let-off rail, and the felt on the spring rail, rest rail and reattached the bridle straps. Polished rest rail, finished Action Prep.

Finished buffing for the Petrof: 2046, I'm not so certain I retired the knot correctly in the cord that wraps around the pedals. I removed the cord to mitigate any further stress, and in concluding my buffing I made an attempt to retie that same cord as it was prior to buffing to the best of my knowledge. Henry Miller: 43064 buffing completed.

Monday, September 22, 2025. KNABE 41783. Routered all new keytops through router bits 3 and 4.
Tuesday, September 23, 2025. Removed all sharps and carefully sanded them, then painted them. Finished project KNABE 41783.
Wednesday, September 24, 2025. EVERETT 37998. I mounted the keys on the keyboard and applied Baking Soda, then numbered them. Removed all the old bushings and installed the new ones up to key 42.
Thursday, September 25, 2025. Finished installing all the new bushings, then carefully removed all the ivory and installed the new keytops up to key 22.
Friday, September 26, 2025. Finished installing all the new keytops and routered bits 3 and 4 up to key 32.

Gulbransen 170677: we are so close. Pedals are clear of metal. Still have a few small things before I say I'm done. There's a small issue on the sustain vacuum uptake when delivering signal. So it seems I shouldn't need more than a day more to finalize the player. Let me know what you decide on both QC and sliding door.  Tunings 1/1

Aeolian 96128: QC finised. I found the fallboard/keyslip hardware with Ben and he said they still need to be sent in for replating so they were not installed. Rest of piano is assembled though. Baldwin Acrosonic 1029243: Bass dampers fixed and QC complete. Louis Zwicki 10149: QC complete. Payson High: Delivered the music room lamps. Maudie Smith: New damper felt installed. Herrburger Brooks 4219: Restringing Done! Just need to bring it up to pitch and I will need the week to continue with DHRT. Tunings 10/10.

Tuning = 2 out of 5, Delivered piano bench to Bountiful and installed cup to hold the shorter lid prop, Knabe 41783: finished action prep except for the few double flanges which I need 3D printed, went back to the teardown, I had just began to measure bass strings when there was an emergency tuning in Spanish Fork,

Walworth 30357: Filed some more holes Monday morning.  Smith & Nixon 20757: Will finish the QC on Monday. Marcello has the cracked key and it should be ready today. The bass string with the loose winding I just stretched the loose part up so it doesn't buzz until we get the replacement.

Bernareggi 19507
Finished installing new hammers
Spaced with heating pliers and aligned hammers 
Started putting the keys on
Keys not fully refinished
Keys extremely tight on front bushings and some front rail pins are angled back causing them to be really tight regardless of bushing
A lot of easing and tapping some front rail pins forward to fix the angle
Weighted and heated keys to try and fix warped fronts
Sanded expanding lead weights on all keys
Leveled white keys
Made new thinner front rail punchings to allow dip and room to adjust for after touch
Fixed some more warped keys
Leveled black keys
Set capstan heights
Straightened out some more warped keys
Regulated let off
Regulated after touch
Started regulating back checks
Straightened out warped keys again
I didn’t see any pedals on the shelf for this piano so I’m not sure about the trapwork situation for this one
I think I’ll be done sometime Monday morning

Young Chang 801927: Done! Couple notes for this piano—the front bushing are the looser side and it's more to difficult to space the keys. There is a missing bracket that attaches from the pitman to the sostenuto rod. I don't know if we want to try to fix or not. Wurlitzer 167280: The QC is taking way longer that usual. I need more time to finish. I am even missing the bottom board for the Wurlitzer. Similar story with Everett 34956, I am missing the pedal rods for that piano

Gulbransen 149314: Started Tuesday and worked on taking of keybed, trapwork and plate  to get to cleaning. Waiting for double Casters to come so we can flip the piano upright, started working on the action, finished removing Dampeners. Estimated time: End of Wednesday (and whenever Casters come).

Smith & Nixon 20757, Tuning done. Apollo 123770, Tuning done. Steinway 214896, Tuning done. Marshall & Wendell 75866, Tuning done. Wurlitzer 167280, Tuning done. (String broke at A7 I believe, and needs replaced.) Everett 34956, Tuning done. Ludwig 23086, Tuning done. Hailun 33P 309868, Tuning done. satin Hailun 121 239862, Tuning done. Young Chang G801927, Tuning done. Burnareggi, buffing done for candle stick holders. Baldwin 106197, Buffing done. Gulbransen 149314, Buffing began on Friday, should have it done Tuesday at the latest.

Monday, June 29, 2025. EVERETT 37998. I finished routing 3 and 4 of all the new key tops, then sanded all the sharps and painted them.
Tuesday, September 30, 2025. I finished project Everett 37998.
Wednesday, January 10, 2025. STARCK 74040. I removed all the sharps from the piano and sanded all the keys one by one, then painted them. I applied three coats of paint because the wood from the sharps was very dry. I finished project STARCK 74040.
Thursday, February 10, 2025. LESTER 185231. I placed all the keys on the key board and applied baking soda. They are numbered below. I removed all the old bushings and installed the new ones up to the 32-key keyboard.
Friday, March 10, 2025. I finished installing all the new bushings, then removed the ivory and salvaged the keys that were in good condition. I installed the new keys tops up to the 63-key keyboard.

Gulbransen 170677: as best as I can tell we are finally done with this player. Adding the feeder bellows made all the difference. Thanks for your help. Knabe 116388: so pleased to report this player is done and amazing. Will need a few hours to remake the vinyl under covering once material arrives. 400 hrs. Zeck 91821: unfortunately the nightmare continues. Have to completely redo the decks that were botched. It will likely be several weeks before I can estimate a time line. Tunings 0/1

Apollo 123770: QC completed. Steinway 214896: QC completed. Sage Canyon Middle School: hammers were catching on the backchecks. I also had to do some whippen to hammer alignment. Katie Carver: put in the fallboard the only way I could see it working. Brinkerhoff 181163: has some perimeter separation as well so I started with that and will do rib separation on Monday. Might need a day for the QC.  Didn't get to the Herrburger Brooks this week. Tunings 7/7.

Tuning = 2 out of 6, Knabe 41783:  measured (and Evan ordered) bass strings (and there were 57 strings, can you believe it?), measured treble strings, completed teardown sheet, removed plate screws & plate, had to carefully take off finished sides of piano to remove plate, cleaned soundboard and strapped piano to the column at location #67, (the soundboard has some splits on the topside but no cracks going all the way through), to be done with this piano I need to rebush the double flanges and attach dampers/hammers, and finally the Key Bed (soda blast/Scotch Brite center & front pins/new Backrail cloth & punchings,

Smith & Nixon 20757: Completed - took all day Monday. Ludwig 23086: I missed this voicing! I spent longer with the smith & nixon and overlooked it Friday. Can I do it Monday? Marshall & Wendell 75866: Didn't get to too much of it - waiting for the tuning. I found the pedals with Ben and told him they were high priority. Baldwin 223532: Completed voicing. This one turned out pretty nice - Jake did a good job. Walsworth 30357: Did more filling and drilling.  **It's the kid's fall break Friday and Monday (and not for Jackie at byu) I put in some alternative hours in the calendar for next weekend to help with the kids during their break and still get shop time.

The two QC's are finally done! Prein 120271: Getting ready to restring! You can give me all week to restring and do DHRT
Gulbransen 149314:Finished up the Action prep, took out plate and put on double Casters. Howard 156792: started to refurbish the piano on Friday, cleaned keys, Keybed and vacuumed trapwork.
Steinway 355193, Tuning done. Hailun 178 313761, Tuning done. Bernareggi 19507, Tuning done. Marshall & Wendell 75866, Tuning done. Vacuum's emptied. Gulbransen 149314, buffing done.

Monday, 10/06/2025. LESTER 185231. I finished installing all the new keytops, then routed them through routers 3 and 4 up to key 44.
Tuesday, 10/07/2025. I finished routing all the keytops through routers 3 and 4, then carefully cleaned all the sharps.
Wednesday, 10/08/2025. I finished project LESTER 185231. GULBRANSEN 149314. I placed the keys on the board and applied Baking Soda. Then I numbered them. Removed all the old bushings.
Thursday, 10/09/2025. I installed the new bushings up to key 54.
Friday, 10/10/2025. I finished installing the new bushings, removed the old keystops, and installed the new ones up to key 39.
Zeck 91821: decks and strikers prepped for recovering. Knabe: 116388: underside cover finished. Tunings 0/2

Brinkerhoff 181168: QC complete. Lacey Bangerter: I brought a new spring that was a little weaker than the 1st replacement spring and added a felt bushing at the sustain arm pivot point where the plastic piece had chipped. Hopefully those things solve the issue long term. Herrburger Brooks 4219: started DHRT. Will need next week to continue this. The hammers and whippens were missing but luckily I found them on the salvage shelf. 9/10 tunings. The one not rescheduled was a school.

Short week for me not feeling well and took Friday off, Tuning = 2 out of 4, Knabe 41783: forgot that I had to fix a problem with the pedals, and there were complications with the 3D printed double flanges but I figured out a solution, and the piano is finally done,

Bernareggi 19507: completed voicing. Marshall & Wendell 75866: completed voicing and QC ***Jake said something about restoring the player system. There are player parts inside that I left be, and there are player parts in the box that I left by the piano - didn't break down the box.  What should I do with those?  Young Chang G801927: completed voicing. Ludwig 23086: completed voicing. Walworth 30357: did some more pin hole drilling. --completed the monthly restoration updates Saturday morning.

Prein 120271: Restringing is nearly complete. I will need Monday to finish and all the rest of the week for DHRT.

Howard 156792: reshaped hammers, repinned, and cleaned action. Tightened all screws on the action, replaced missing dampers, cleaned strings and replaced felt punchings on the Keybed along with back rail felt. Korban has the metal hardware, keys need new bushings. Squared hammers to strings (will do it again after keys are put in). Still needs regulation.

Baldwin 223532, Tuning done. Decker 4870, Tuning done. Wurlitzer 167280, Tuning done. Astin Weight 8017, Tuning done. Kimball 176797, Tuning done. Mason & Hamlin 37957, Tuning done. Bush & Lane 17827, Tuning done. Plate dusted. white Samick IJC01114, Tuning done. Hailun 178 313357, Tuning done. Wurlitzer 1293074, Tuning done. Vacuum Maintenance completed. Steinway 38930, I've gone through most of this. I cannot find the external  lyre pedal rods, and I don't see any lyre support rods. I spent some time Friday buffing the metal in the cheek locks that guide it into place, and one of them ended up being coppered and would need replated. I'm currently working on buffing the agraffes, and should have those done on Monday. I also am tumbling the pedals for Howard 156792, and I hope to have them done this week.

Monday, 10/13/2025, GULBRASEN 149314. Finished installing all the new bushings, then finished installing all the new key tops.
Tuesday, 10/14/2025. Router es 3 and 4 all the new key tops.
Wednesday, 10/14/2025. Carefully sanded all the sharps and painted them. Completed project GULBRANSEN Thursday, October 16, 2025. Mathushek 8409. Removed the damaged key tops and installed new ivory pieces. Repaired the damaged fronts and replaced the broken ones.
Friday, October 17, 2025. Continue repairing the damaged parts and fronts up to key 45.

Zeck 91821: strikers done and attached to wind chest. Valve body cleaned and tested - seems good. So far I'm unable to locate pictures useful for player reference. Have a puzzle to put together this week on the whole upper body. Tunings 0/1

Alina Simpson: Sustain pedal just needed to be adjusted and there were some bobbing hammers. Kyle & Felicia: saw right away that the sticking key was a key slip issue so I fixed that. Not sure how it was missed in previous warranty visits. Herrburger Brooks 4219: currently working on the hammers. I noticed we don't have a full set of shanks so I may need another project on Monday while I wait for the shanks. I believe they have been ordered. Tunings 8/9 tunings.

Tuning= 3 out of 9, Ludwig 23086: went thru QC, wasn't thrilled with regulation (all the let off screws were down as far as they could go and still needed more, lost motion was way off, and had to ease a lot of the keys,and just an observation… the   finish on the knee board is developing a faint white patch. I think Julio calls this cancer, this is not a problem unless the customer is extremely picky, I put it all back together and thought I was done until I started playing it, nearly half the keys stay down when the sustain pedal is engaged, I can still fix it with some more easing, but I don't want overdo it, Jake said it looks like there's too much bushing cloth in the holes, I left the Ludwig as is and moved on to restringing the Rudolf 22795,
Walworth 30357: working on the restringing- the epoxy is working well. Will need both days next week.

Leveled and sqaured keys. Started regulating. A few more days are needed to complete.

Everett 34956, Tuning done. Ludwig 23086, Tuning done. Hallet Davis 31849, Tuning done. Yamaha U1 4404193, Tuning done. Ludwig 79272, Wednesday, Tuning done. Full Regulation Needed. Yamaha C7 2750092, Tuning done. Irmler grand 12992, Tuning done. Yamaha Console Mahogany 1654004, Tuning done. 6 White Laminate shelving units were disassembled of their mounting brackets, and their brackets were placed in the attic, and the shelving units themselves were placed within  the cabinetry storage room. Railings on the rear stairs were cleaned and dusted. Ceiling tiles that weren't in their proper positions throughout the building were adjusted into place best as possible. Lightbulb inventory taken and sent to Brigham. Howard: 156792, pedals were sanded and buffed for putting this piano together. Steinway 38930, agraffes buffed, and inventory taken.

Monday, October 20, 2025. MATHUSHEK 8409. Finished replacing all fronts.
Tuesday, October 21, 2025. Finished replacing damaged parts, then cleaned and polished all ivory key tops.
Wednesday, October 22, 2025. Taked photos to send to the client. Finished project MATHUSHEK 8409.
Thursday, October 23, 2025. BERNAREGGI 19507. Carefully removed all the old ivory and deliver it to Melisa. Installed the new key tops.
Friday, October 24, 2025. Routered all new key tops using 3 and 4, then sanded to a 12.

Zeck 91821: upper body including transmission , tracker, and wind motor almost complete for testing and reinstalling. I still have several auxiliaries to restore and hopefully nachos recovering of the bellows doesn't need reworking. I think at the end of this next week I may be making music. Or at least have a clearer view of time requirements. Tunings 0/1

Showroom: Astin Weight 8017 completed. Promberger 2263 complete. Sherman Clay G015099 complete. Herrburger Brooks 4219: finished the hammers and currently aligning to strings. Will need next week to finish this. I did not work on any of the PRSB. Tunings 7/8

Tuning: 2 out of 6, Ludwig 23086: after discovering that strengthening the jack springs solved most of the problem, I eased the sticking keys, and now the piano is ready, Rudolf 23795: installed plate & felts, and by Friday I was in the zone (restringing a pair in under 4 minutes, Yay!
Walworth 30357: two notes left to finish the treble strings, then only bass strings left. Will finish Monday.

Yamaha T175553: complete. Hailun 1P 251000: complete. Emily Duncan (Lehi HS): was missing bumpers for the fallboard to rest on so it was causing a buzz. Tuning = 4 out of 6, did an in-home appraisal and completed paperwork, had some significant drive time this week, Schaeffer 43165: completed QC (backchecks not checking, fixed them, a dozen or so keys stayed down when sustain pedal was depressed, but I was able to make jack springs springier and ease the keys a little more), Rudolf 22795: nearly done with restringing and getting faster all the time; however, the shared hitch pins were not indicated, and I had to redo several pairs, which was disappointing,
Walworth 30357: finished restringing. Did a chip tuning. Just need to add some stringing ribbon then will be finished.

There are only tiny cracks that might be good to just fill some ca glue. Pulled pins from top of treble bridge - will just replace that section.

Bernareggi 19507: been on this QC all week running the circle of circles. Still have regulation issues and at about 1pm Friday Curtis informed me that this piano may need to be regulated with less dip. The sharps look high. Overall I am having so many bobbling hammers. It is my recommendation that a true expert carries this one to the finish. I don't want to feel like I'm wasting time and money. I'm guessing my detachment from regulation over the last year, and this unique springless action is the cause of my trepidation. Call me if you have more questions please. DORIS

Summary: Wing 39450-finish 100% ( without Dical) . Weber 43336- finish lacquered soundboard.  Kimball 327997- polish only piano 95%.  Howard 156792- put Dical , full assembly 100%.
entries.text: Monday: Colby 30776- Sprayed 1st copat lacquer all piano. Kimball 327997- Special sanded with water for polish.  Emerson 56874- Rebuilding, Chequed jack flange, change piano carters. Schimmel nelson 1077- bass chek block repaired, installed rubber bumpers.  Tuesday: Colby 30776- Sanded ( twice) Sprayed 2nd and 3st coat laquered all piano. Emerson 56874: Rebuilding- Chequed jack flange. Wednesday: Colby 30776- Full Assembly.( without Dical) Kimball 327997-Special sanded. Emerson 56874- Rebuilding. Finish Chequed jack flange. Thursday: Kimball 327997- Stayed polish. Steinway 154950: laser, sanded. Bench Colby: Sanded. Friday: Henry Miller 43064- Sanded and sprayed 2 coat lacquered only Soundboard. Steinway 154950- Sanded. Emerson 56874-Rebuilding- Replace felts on whippens, Replaced all let off buttons, Installed let off rail.  Summary: Schimmel Nelson 1077: finish.  Hallet Davis 39365: 50%  Kimball 327997: 90%  Howard 15679292: 100% ( Waiting Dical and hinges.) Colby 30776: 100% ( without Dical) Steinway 154950:25% Henry Miller 43064- 100%( soundboard).

Walworth 30357
Finished after touch
Regulated spoons
Set back checks
Pedals not finished so left off trap work
Rudolf 22795
Installed keybed
Really loose strings so chip tuned up by ear for tension
Aligned dampers
Installed dampers
Regulated damper lift timing
Numbered and marked bore distance for new hammers
Bored hammers
Tapered bass hammers
Started Glueing hammers on shanks
Swapped out pedals on Colby 30776
Finished glueing hammers on shanks
Started setting hammer heights

Kurtzmann 92422: Teardown almost complete. Reglued one of the legs that was coming off. Plate is removed, all cabinetry is upstairs. Action is ready, except for hammer rest rail that still needs new felt. New hammer butts and let off buttons placed. Steinway 154950: Action is ready with new hammer butts.
Marshall & Wendell 75866: Everything on the is starting to come back together on the decks. I continue on Monday.

Knabe 128985: Finished Restringing Put on the string felt.
Baldwin H 41826: put plate in adjusted Screws, and started gluing understring Felt.

Samick 8510474, Tuning done. Geyer 170 81891376, Tuning done, needs regulation work, hammer alignment, etc. Astin Weight 8017, Tuning done. Herrburger Brooks 4219, Tuning done. Wang & Sons 160 81891390, Tuning done. Hailun 178 313357, Tuning done. Geyer 123 61691851, Tuning done. Walworth 30357, Tuning done. Acrosonic 343593, Tuning done. The Following have been prepped and ready to be sent for replating: Emerson        56874
Wick        29908
Steinway        62974
Krieter        16404
Weber        33045
Gulbransen        149314
Cunningham        16124
Decker Bros        20349
Walworth        30357
Steinway        154950
        
Steinway        140204
Kimball        148345
Lester 39753, I'm working in action. Mason & Hamlin 7912, part 1A is complete. The hammers and let-off button for action are pending. I fixed the caster on Lester's piano.

Monday, December 8, 2025. EMERSON 56874. Finished installing all the new key tops, then routed them through routers 3 and 4 up to key 56.
Tuesday, December 9, 2025. Finished routing all the keys through routers 3 and 4. Carefully sanded all the sharps.
Wednesday, December 10, 2025. I did not work today with supervisor's permission.
Thursday, December 11, 2025. I painted all the sharps. I finished project EMERSON 56874. BALDWIN 10697. I puted the keys on the panel and applied baking soda.
Friday, December 12, 2025. I numbered all the keys, removed all the old bushings, and then puted in the new ones. I am waiting for a response from the owner of WALWORTH 30357, regarding whether the ivory will be retained or if we will replace the key tops with new plastic ones.

Henry Miller 43064: some rib separation, 21 shims. Finished and taken to sanding. My understand was Dorris would coat it Friday. Steinway 154950: replaced bass bridge cap. There is neg DB throughout so I will be recapping the tenor also.

PSRB: still working on the Cunningham. Will need next week to continue these projects. Herrburger Brooks 4219: QC pretty much complete. I need an hour or so on Monday to look for that buzzing but I think I’ve addressed everything else. I noticed that towards the bass end the wood on the key sticks aren’t hid by the fallboard. I don’t think we did new keytops so I’m not sure what’s going on. 7/9 Tunings

Tunings 1 out of 9, 2 warranty repairs, Samick KJJH00112: completed refurbishing (I reshaped the hammers, and it would do well with a quick voicing if Curtis has the time), Baldwin Acrosonic 784967: did some deep cleaning, reshaping hammers now,

Petrof 2046: Almost finished. Shims are all done, and sanded flat. The sound board sounds good! Filled in a few cracks in the pin block.  Drilling tuning pin holes in progress. M&H 7912: not yet started.

entries.tech:
DORIS

Summary: Wing 39450-finish 100% ( without Dical) . Weber 43336- finish lacquered soundboard.  Kimball 327997- polish only piano 95%.  Howard 156792- put Dical , full assembly 100%.
entries.text: Monday: Wing 39450- Laser, sanded.  Bench Colby- Sprayed stain and lacquer.  Emerson 56874-Rebuilding- Lubricated action put clp and intalled, Attach bridle straps, Put action back in storage together with rest rail and spring rail. Tuesday: Wing 39450- Laser,sanded.  Emerson 56874-Rebuilding- Measured all the strings. Bench Gulbransen, repair and sprayed polyurethane. Wednesday: Wing 39450- Laser, sanded, repair bench. Bench colby, sprayed lacquer. Emerson 56874-Rebuilding- Removed all wound strings. Thursday: Wing 39450- Sanded. Steinway 154950- sanded. Emerson 56874- Rebuilding- Removed tuning pin, Removed each screw. Friday: Wing 39450- laser only piano, sanded. Emerson 56874: Rebuilding- Removed the plate, replaced the casters. Stored purchases of screws. Saturday: Wing 39450- Sanded.  SUMMARY: Wing 39450 : 40%  Hallet davis 39365: 50%( without color) Kimball 327997: 90%  Howard 156792 ( Dical has not arrived). Steinway 154950: 30%.  Emerson 56874 Rebuilding: 100%.

Rudolf 22795
Started Installing hammers
Went to help pick up a piano in Spanish fork
Finished installing hammers
Spaced and squared hammers
Put keys on piano
Fixed some crooked jacks that were hitting neighbor hammer butts
Repinned one of the bottom flanges that wouldn’t stay tight
Keys sitting way too high before any leveling so swapping balance rail punchings for thinner size
Leveled keys
Set blow distance, a little wonky since rest rail seems to be a bit crooked
Set capstans 
Regulated let off
Regulated after touch
Set back checks
Trapwork seemed like it was already done so just slipped hooks through pedals to lift them
Shimmel 1077
Reregulated let off
Shimmed key slip
Took out action to try and fix clicking notes
Noticed the damper that was having issues has a severely cracked flange
Replaced flange
Almost every jack spring is misaligned and tricky to fix

Henry F Miller 43064 Treble strings done, but it still needs bass strings. I was waiting on felt for the Kurtzmann 92422 hammer rest rail, which got delivered this week. I'll put it on first thing on Monday morning.
Wing 39450: Done! Marshall & Wendell 75866: Player still in progress. I will be taking the 26th off and also the 31st and January 1st off.
Baldwin 41826: still restringing need 1more day

Colby 30776, Tuning done. Hailun 7P 309868, Tuning done. HD 121 2739386, Tuning done. Kimball 148345, Steinway 140204, the rest of the metal hardware has been found. Vacuum Maintenance was done. Howard 72172, completed. Cunningham 16124, pedal mounting brackets cleaned on the wire wheel. Kreiter 16404, pedal mounting brackets cleaned on the wire wheel. Emerson 56874, pedal mounting brackets cleaned with the wire wheel, and pedal adjustment rods buffed and cleaned.

Lester 39753, finished the action. Mason & Hamlin 7912, The hammers and let-off button for action have been installed, I just need to check some details with Jake on Monday and it will be ready. Regarding the cleaning of the tuning pins on the Mason piano, the process is slow and 40% has been completed.

Monday, December 15, 2025. BALDWIN 106195. I cleaned and polished the keytops; they were kept because they were in good condition.

Tuesday, December 16, 2025. I sanded all the sharps and then painted them. I finished project Baldwin 106197. WERNER 37854. (This piano is not listed in the ISOLVED app.) I removed the keys from the piano, placed them on the board, applied baking soda, and then numbered them.

Wednesday, December 17, 2025. I removed the old bushings and installed the new ones up to the 62nd key.
Thursday, December 18, 2025. I finished installing all the bushings, then removed the ivory and installed the new keycaps up to the 26th key. I put this project on hold because Jake is giving me WING 39450 keycaps.
Friday, December 19, 2025. WING 39450. I installed the keycaps on the keyboard and applied baking soda, then numbered them. I removed the old bushings and installed the new ones up to the 43rd key.
Steinway 154950: tenor bridge recapped and moved to sanding. Lester 39753: I will be doing a full service on this piano. I estimate a little less than a week. Have the bridges chopped so far.
Cunningham 16124: almost done. Just need a day to clean up the bridges and glue the base bridge back on. I didn’t get to the other PRSB projects but can start those Tuesday. 7/11 Tunings.
Tuning = 3 out of 11, Baldwin Acrosonic 784967: replaced 9 missing springs on the Spring Rail, reglued some damper felts,

Continued work on Lester 66823. Removed old player set up and coordinated with Matthew about parts to recycle from the old player. Removed cabinetry of piano, labels and set aside on correct shelving. Removed old casters and installed new casters. Glued split and cracked piano base board, and sanded it down. Shined piano lever rods and installed new felt. Separated and labeled screws into separate ziplock baggies and placed in box. Removed keys and made sure the number order was written so it was readable. Placed keys on their own box on proper shelf. Removed key board and did baking soda blast replacing felt. Measured piano bass strings and treble strings filling out the proper paperwork. Removed piano strings and tuning rods. Created board for proper removal of plate screws.

Training Jacob and Ari. Petrof 2046: Very close - drilled out at 1/4 in. Will drill the rest of the way for size 2 pins, will go fast. Walworth 30357 (73): completed voicing 
Samick KJJH00112 (122): Didn't make it to this voicing, will complete Monday. — planning on working Monday and Friday next week.

Shimmel 1077
Aligned jack springs
Reinstalled action
Squared hammer that was rubbing after tightening screws
Bosendorfer 9374
Started tear down
Labeled shelf and put cabinetry on it
Measured bass strings
Measured treble strings
Removed strings
Started removing plate screws

Wyman 570919599: Started Refurbish, vacuumed out piano lubricated trapwork, started working on the action. checked pinning on hammers and most needed re-pinning. I started re-pinning and I've almost finished. Henry F. Miller 43064 is restrung, and has new felt. I wasn't sure if I should start putting it back together
Marshall & Wendell 75866: Still in progress. I will be late on Friday the 2nd. I will be back around 8:30am
Story & Clark 900975, Tuning done. Rudolf 22795, Tuning done. Hailun 178 313761, Hailun 178 313761, Tuning done. Hailun 178 313357, Tuning done.

Mason & Hamlin is finished. Regarding the cleaning of the tuning pins on the Mason piano, the process is slow and 60% has been completed. Ellington 52031, I started working on the action, as there was little space to work with the piano.

Monday, December 22, 2015. I finished installing all the new bushings, then carefully removed the ivory from the keytops up to the 38th key.
Tuesday, December 23, 2025. I finished removing all the ivory, salvaging all the pieces that were in good condition, then installed the new keytops up to the 65th key.
Wednesday, December 24, 2025. I finished installing all the new keytops, then routed them on a 3 and 4 saw up to the 69th key.
Thursday, December 25, 2025. Today is Christmas Day (holiday).
Friday, December 26, 2025. I finished routing all the keytops on a 3 and 4 saw, then sanded all the sharp edges and painted them. I finished project WING 39450. I resumed project WERNER 27854, which had been pending since last week.
Lester 39753: P - R - S all done. Plate is currently in piano for setting bridge height.

Howard 404728: QC done. Had a lot of loose hammers to reglue. Hailun 121 309130: QC complete. Cunningham 16124: finishing up the new apron. The bridges look good enough to just clean up and maybe glue some of the small cracks. Will need all next week to continue the PRSB projects. 1/3 tunings.
Baldwin Acrosonic

Rudolf 22795: completed voicing. Samick KJJH00112: completed voicing. M&H 7912: rib separation in progress. The bridges on this one were coming separated from the sound board as well. I took the screws of the back and popped the bridges off to reglue after fixing the sound board cracks.

Monday: Wing 39450- Installed decal  Kimball 327997: Pulished legs, crack repair on the lid. Steinway 154950- Sanded piano and soundboard. Tuesday: Kimball 327997: Sprayed other coat lacquered pieces. Steinway 15950: Sanded . Walworth 30357- Laser. Wednesday: Kimball 327997- crack repai on the lid, pulished something pieces, Steinway 154950- I applied paint remover and cleaned with thinner to remove embedded white apots of old white paint. Friday: Steinway 154950- applied remove and cleaned pieces, put stain piano and something pieces, sprayed lacquered soundboard. Walworth 30357- laser, sanded.  Summary:  Wing 39450: full finish 100%.  Kimball 327997: 95%  ( assembly only piano with legs) Steinway 154950: 60%  Walworth 30357: 30%  Hallet Davis 39365: 50%.

Walworth 30357
Adjust let off rail positioning
Removed rest rail for easier access to hammers
Repinned and refused a lot of hammers
Did so many in High treble section that the spacing and alignment got thrown off quite a bit
Re aligned and squared hammers with heated pliers and lighter
Rest rail changed slightly when putting back in which caused lost motion
Adjusted capstans 
Regulated let off
Bosendorfer 
Removed final plate screws
Took action out and set on table nearby
Remove blocks covering edge of plate
Roughed in shape and size of replacement blocks

Wyman 570919599: Completed regulation following instructions out of the book.  If at all possible I would like to be present for the QC on the regulation for this piano to see where I can make improvements. This was something more challenging for me, and I'd like to learn how i can improve.
Marshall  & Wendell 75866: Still a work in progress. Give me another week to continue

Howard 156792, Tuning done. Hailun 161 313723, Tuning done. Geyer 61691852, Tuning done and bench set up behind the piano. Wing 39450, Tuning done. Yamaha C3 3250506, Tuning done. Johannes Seiler Grand IKNFG0114, Tuning done. nickel trim HD 148 2707439, Tuning done. Astin Weight 8017, Tuning done. Vacuum filter cleaned on Monday, and I installed key led weights on the Wing and Sons 39450, and I had finished buffing the parts for the bosendorfer

Regarding the cleaning of the tuning pins on the Mason piano, the process is slow and 60% has been completed. Ellington 52031, the action is complete.  Regarding part 1A, only the strings and the plate need to be removed. The parts where the wheels go need glue; I'm gluing one now and it will be ready on Monday.

Monday, December 29, 2025. WERNER 27854. I finished installing all the key tops, then routed them through routers 3 and 4 up to key 20.
Tuesday, December 30, 2025. I finished routing all the key tops through routers 3 and 4, then carefully sanded all the old sharps and painted them. Project WERNER 27854 was completed.
Wednesday, December 31, 2025. WALWORTH 30357. I removed all the old key tops and installed the new ones.
Thursday, January 1, 2026. New Year's Day holiday.
Friday, January 2, 2025. I routed all the new key tops through routers 3 and 4. Project WALWORTH 30357 was completed.

Lester 39753: all bridges recapped, moved to sanding. Kreiter 16404: this piano needs about a week. Neg. DB on all bridges. I currently have the bridges chopped and new caps glued. I'm gonna try to get it done quick!

Cunningham 16124: need to bring the bass bridge down as it has too much down bearing. Should only need a day or two on this to glue the bass bridge on. Emerson 56874: Perimeter and rib separation fixed. Just need to take the screws out and put dowels in. Will need all next week to continue with PRSB projects. Tunings 4/6

Mason & Hamlin 7912: fixed a lot of rib separation. Can start cracks and bridges Monday. ***the refinishing shop is working on stripping the finish on this one. They let me take the sound board back to work on it. If they need to work on it next week, I will need a different project while they have it. The sound board will take a while longer.  Knabe 128695: this one is all finished up and Joseph strung it already.
Summary:  Kimball 327997- finish paint friday cover. 95%.  Steinway 154950- sprayed 2 coat lacquer. 75%.Walworth 30357, finish sanded- 50%. Mason Hamlin 7912- laser, sanded 10%.

Bosendorfer 
Removed another block pinning the plate down
Removed plate
Roughed in replacement blocks a bit more and screwed them down
Weber 43336
Installed plate
Put on new felts
Struggled to find capo and v bar for a while but eventually found them
Got stuck screw out of bolt hole after a while
Started restringing
Finished restringing 
Chipped

Wyman 570919599: knee board and front cabinetry have broken plastic clips. other than that, I rechecked the regulation for this piano with Curtis. Henry F Miller 43064: Replaced Dampers, new dampers glued in place. checked damper regulation. started working hanging hammers. Hammers are bored and tapered. New shanks glued into hammers. I still need to hang hammers on the action
Marshall & Wendell 75866: Coming along! Another week down and another week to continue. I have a dentist appointment on Wednesday so I will be late that day.

Werner 27854, Tuning done. Baldwin Hamilton T80555, Tuning done. Kawai 2006957, piano cleaned and hardware buffed. Kawai 690265, Tuning done. Steinway 121109, Tuning done. walnut Hailun French leg 121 278040, Tuning done. Hailun 198 313769, Tuning done. 1973 Yamaha Console #B1654004, Tuning done. Hailun 7P 309868: tuning for delivery tomorrow, Tuning done. Acrosonic 784967: tuning and move to 106, Tuning done, and piano moved. All received plating materials inventoried and put in their respective bins. I used the carpet cleaner on the carpet on the second story nearest the piano's on the wood landing at the top of the stairs. I also put all the Christmas Trees upstairs in the red bags. I buffed the bridge caps, I believe that's what they're called, for the Mason and Hamlin 7912, I'll buff the pedals, and continue improving as I work along.

Regarding the cleaning of the tuning pins on the Mason piano, the process has been completed. Ellington 52031,on Monday I was gluing two legs and istalled the castiers. Everett 397988: Insatlled plate, (I learned about downbearing, and I had to remove the plate once to correct the details and reinstall it), put on new felts, instaled teh capo and pulish screws. Stared restringing, Curtis helped with this part and we will continue on Monday.

Monday, January 5, 2026. KNABE 128695. I placed the keys on the keypad and applied baking soda, then numbered them. I removed all the old bushings and installed the new ones up to key 45.
Tuesday, January 6, 2026. I finished installing the new bushings, then carefully removed the ivory, salvaging the pieces in good condition.
Wednesday, January 7, 2026. I installed all the new key tops and routed them on routers 1 and 2 up to key 11.
Thursday, January 8, 2026. I finished routing all the new key tops on routers 1 and 2, then carefully sanded all the sharp edges and painted them. Project KNABE 128695 was complete.
Friday, January 9, 2026. RUDOLF 22795. I removed all the old keys tops with router 1 and put in all the new keys tops, then I passed them through router 1 and 2 up to key 11.
Kreiter 16404: all bridges recapped. Moved to sanding.

HD 170 2705197, Hailun 7P 309868, Yamaha B1654004: all complete. PRSB: Cunningham 16124: Original bass bridge was warped so I decided to rebuild it so I'm on that process now. Emerson 56874: About ready to work on the soundboard cracks. Will need the rest of next week to continue PRSB projects. 5/7 tunings.

1970s Kimball: fixed hammers. Couldn't find the serial number. A couple dates in the action show the action was probably made in 1976. It probably had some work done on it in '85. Could the serial number have been lacquered over in '85? M&H 7912: Rib separation finished. Made template for bridges. They have this in the refinishing shop, moved to other projects. Lester 66823: rib separation all finished. Ellington 52031: Ari needed a project after finishing his and I trained him on rib separation on this piano. Ribs almost finished on this one. Will move on to cracks next week.

Monday: Steinway 154950- Sanding ,sprayed 3coat lacquered.Hallet Davis 39365- cleaned mineral spirit. Encimera de Madera one coat Waterloo. Tuesday: Hallet Davis 39365- put stain, sprayed 1 coat lacquered. Kreiter 16404- sanding, sprayed  2 coat lacquered. Steinway 154950- cover all pieces and store. Encimera- one coat of Waterlox. Wednesday: Hallet Davis 39365- sanding, sprayed 2nd coat lacquered.Encimera one coat of Waterlox. Thursday: Hallet Davis 39365- full assembly. kimball 327997- polish stand music. Encimera- put one coat of Waterlox. Friday: kimball 1970- laser, sanding. Saturday: kimball 327997- finish polish but have details that can repair Curtis. kimball 1970- laser, sanding.   Summary: kimball 327997- 100% detail for repair. Steinway 154950: 100%.  Walworth 30357: 45%.  Hallet Davis: 100%.  Mason Hamlin:  40%.  Encimera de madera : 100% ( have 7 coat Waterlox). Kimball 1970's : 20%. Kreiter 16404: 100%.

Chickering 8’
Replaced broken hitch pins 
Painted new hitch pins
Put on new felts, couldn’t do all cause we don’t have long enough pieces
Went to start restringing but couldn’t because agraffe holes were plugged with some kind of paint or epoxy
Took awhile but was able to reopen agraffe holes
Started restringing
Initial Torque going fine for the most part until one hole in the second section was extremely loose, all the holes around it seem fine
Epoxied some more cracks in the sound board

Henry F Miller 43064: hammers hung squared and spaced. started regulation. during regulation i noticed lots of broken parts. I Replaced whippens, Jacks and flanges. squared spaced and leveled keys. regulated let off except for on the hammers that were broken. while squaring the hammers I broke a few that needed to be replaced. lastly I'm. not sure what to do with the sostenuto mechanism
Baldwin 41826: In progress. Currently regulating and will need another three or four more days to finish. Rudolph 22795: I do need another couple hours to finish the QC and assembly of the piano

Astin Weight: 8017, Tuning done. Needs another pin block treatment. Geyer 160 81891390: clean the exterior with polish, done. Geyer 150 81891556, Tuning done. Rudolf 22795, Tuning done. Colby 30776, Tuning done. Mason and Hamlin 7912 Buffing done. Ivers and Pond 72472, buffing done.
Everett 397988: I continue working in step 11 (restring). The process has been slow for me since it's my first job of kind.

Monday, January 12, 2026. RUDOLF 22795. Finished routing all the new keytops through routers 3 and 4.

Tuesday, January 13, 2026. Installed the keys on the piano. Finished project RUDOLF 22795.
LESTER 39753. I placed the keys on the keyboard and applied baking soda, then numbered them. Removed the old bushings up to key 42.
Wednesday, January 14, 2026. Finished removing the old bushings and installed the new ones up to key 45.
Thursday, January 15, 2026. Finished installing the new bushings, then cleaned and sanded the ivory keytops with fine wet/dry sandpaper up to key 34.
Friday, January 16, 2026. I finished cleaning and sanding all the ivory keytops, then carefully polished them. I removed the damaged pieces and gave them to Melissa in a labeled bag, and repaired all the loose and defective parts.
Kimball 148345: all bridges recapped. Moved to sanding que. Knabe 41783: barely tipped it on it back. Needs at least two sections of bridge replaced and medium crack repair.

The Ruth Theater warranty: tightened the lid support rod hinge to stop buzzing and pedal squeak resolved. Nancy Algarra warranty: couple bobbing hammers. PRSB: Cunningham 16124: complete and let Julio know. Emerson 56874: noticed the treble bridge is separating from the soundboard in several places so I’ll address that. Will need all next week to continue PRSB. 5/8 Tunings. Also, I'll be taking February 19-23rd off.

P.S. Wick 14540: Completed deep clean. Mostly just shined up visible metals and cleaned out all the groves. It really just needed some spot vacuuming. - Everett 37998: Reassembled keybed/arms, tracked down scatteres boxes (were on multiple unrelated shelves). Dampers installed, first timing pass done. Hammers prepped, bored, and bass hammers tapered. Ran out of shanks after completing the bass section, so I'll need to wait for more shanks to arrive to continue that process. Will install bass hammers on Monday

Cleaned piano sound board and back of piano with tsp. Cradled action and remover hammer rest rail. I tried to replace the felt on the hammer rest rail but found that we did not have any in stock. I recorded that we needed to order them on the white board. I soda blasted the action, removed the button rail and removed the hammer butts numbering the necessary hammers and keeping them in the piano box. I began removing wippens re-gluing jacks. Finished re-gluing jacks and began installing new hammer butts. Finished installing new hammer butts and made sure

Monday: Walworth 30357- Cleaned mineral spirit, Put stain, Put Shellac( have fish eyes). Kimball 1970's- Laser, Sanding.  Tuesday: Walworth 30357- Sanding( twice) sprayed 1st and 2nd coat Laquered.  Mason Hamlin 7912- Cleaned mineral spirit.. Kimball 1970's- Laser, sanding. Wednesday: Walworth 30357-Sanding, sprayed 3st coat lacquered, Assembly. Kimball 1970's- sanding. Thursday: Mason Hamlin 7912- Put Shellac, Sprayed 1st coat Lacquered with fish eyes drops, Sanding. Kimball 1970's- Sanding. Friday: Mason Hamlin 7912- Sanding, Make up, Sprayed 3st coat laquered only piano and Side Arm. Kimball 1970"s Sanding.  Saturday: Kimball 148345- Sanding Soundboard.   Summary: Walworth 30357: 100% ( without Decal, Curtis). Mason Hamlin 7912: 85%. Kimball 1970"s: 50%. Kimball 148345: 50% Soundboard.

Chickering 8’
Added more epoxy because it had sank down into the cracks
Weber 43336
Installed keybed
Installed action
Replaced two missing damper heads
Difficult to find away for the damper heads between the bass and treble section to coexist
Schimmel 1077
Installed spring under sustain pedal
Realigned hammer that started rubbing again
Put cabinetry back on and polished
Chickering 8’
Sanded and lacquered filled cracks
Weber 43336
Started prepping dampers while waiting for Chickering lacquer to dry
Chickering 8’
Continue restringing 
Finished restringing except for epoxied hole
Baldwin 41826: Done! Knabe 116388: Got started this afternoon and will need next week to continue. All other items are also done!

Schimmel & Nelson 1077, Tuning done. Mason & Hamlin 37957, Tuning done. Geyer 160 81891380, Tuning needs to be done, this piano was still in its box.  Yamaha U1 4359798, Tuning done. Yamaha U3 4188544, Tuning done. Yamaha UX1 4536832, Tuning done. Yamaha YU1 5950342, Tuning done. Ivers and Pond 72472, buffing done. Ellington 52031 buffing done. Steinway 3536 buffing of hinges done, the pedals, lock, and striker need to be replated. Baldwin 41826, began buffing on Friday, should finish by Tuesday.
Everett 397988: I finished step 11 (restring). I started working in piano Baldwin 106197. Now I working in step 11.

Monday, January 19, 2026. LESTER 39753. I carefully sanded all the sharps and then painted them. Project LESTER 39753 completed.
Tuesday, January 20, 2026. KNABE 116388. I removed the keyboard from the piano, placed the keys on the board and applied baking soda, then numbered them, removed all the old bushings and installed the new ones up to key 50.
Wednesday, January 21, 2026. I finished installing the new bushings, then sanded all the sharps and painted them.
Thursday, January 22, 2026. I gently cleaned all the ivory keytops. Project KNABE 116388 completed.
STEINWAY 154950. I placed the keys on the board and applied baking soda, then numbered them. I removed all the old bushings and installed the new ones up to the 65th key.
Friday, January 23,2026. I finished installing all the new bushings, then removed all the old key tops and installed the new ones.
Knabe 41783: all cracks, ribs finished. Almost finished setting bridge height. 2 more days. Add mathushek. 2-3 days.

Warranty work: Trenton Romph- One side of the key frame was closer to the key slip than the other so keys were catching on the key slip. There were also a lot of loose screws, mainly hammer flange screws so I tightened them. Leona Hanna- muffler rail installed and adjusted. Emerson 56874: hoping to finish this by Tuesday. Steinway 181349: Action prep mostly complete. Waiting for more long let off screws, new hammer butts, and two new wippens. Let off screws and hammer butts were ordered before Doris left, wippens were not. This action had a lot of weird rabbit holes to go down, so it took longer than expected to get it to a point I was happy with. - Hallet Davis 22947: Teardown in progress. Cabinetry removed, all string measurements taken, bass strings removed. A problem I would appreciate input on is the issue of removing the plate. Curtis and I both spend several minutes trying to figure out how to remove the plate without causing damage, but I'm the end we agreed that the only way to do so is to remove the feet and sides, as the plate is significantly underneath them. Unfortunately, the feet and sides are glued and would have to be broken off. As this would almost definitely cause damage to the finish, I don't want to be the one to make the final call on it. My biggest concern is the sides cracking/splitting instead of coming away from the body at the seams, which I worry would damage the wood grain veneers. If I were being overabundantly cautious, I'd consider removing the veneer panels, just in case, but I also don't know what that entails.

Hallet Davis 22947: Sides and pinblock had to be removed to be able to remove the plate. This turned out to be quite the undertaking, and we were not able to completely avoid damage. The bass side in particular has some significant damage through one of the veneers and the bottom edge had separated from the side. The pinblock is not damaged, but will require some work to be able to reattach (removing/cutting flush the screws we had to cut through, removing/replacing the wooden dowels that pulled out of their holes, and smoothing/leveling the faces of both the pinblock and the part of the body it attaches to). I don't know if that's something you want me to do, or if it'd be better for whoever does the board work to do it. Trapwork soda blasted/cleaned and all felts and leathers replaced. Keybed soda blasted, and all but one bushing has been replaced, so it's almost done.

Lester 39753: completed voicing. There were some buzzing bass strings - it was not enough of a Z angle where it went across the bridge, went over the bridge too straight. I pounded the pins over to either side and it fixed the issue. Mason & Hamlin 7912: completed bass and treble bridge. Just have tenor bridge left to drill, chisel, and insert pins. — also helped Courtney get the sides off her piano. One side came off really well. The other was really stubborn and took a long time. Then we discovered the plate actually went underneath the pinblock. I helped get that off as well, which ended up difficult because of hidden screws holding the pinblock we had to cut through after partially separating.

Knabe 42129: Bass string sheet completed and in turn in. Teardown sheet completed and in piano box. The agraffes are quite mismatched. 8 of the originals have already broken (6 previously replaced and 2 currently broken). It may be worth it to replace them all. The 22 most treble agraffes were also cut flush on one side to meet the edge of the plate. Downbearing was all bad, with the exception of a small section at highest treble. Matt agrees on recommending a full recapping. NOTE: This piano will be complicated to string. It is majority tied strings, has hitch pins fully covered by plate struts, and strings passing through a second bass bridge. - Decker 20349: Ozoned overnight. I did a ton of scraping and blasting to remove the worst of the urine build up. I'm a bit worried the bottom board wont be salvageable, just going off of how swollen and rotting it is. Several of the screws are so corroded that they can actually be soda blasted away in places. Luckily the trapwork is in excellent condition. It doesn't seem like the cats got inside the piano at all. I'm also not sure the Knee board and the (I cannot for the life of me remember the actual name of this) the board the pedals go through are salvageable, tho I think they're more likely to still have good wood inside than the bottom board is. The piano is still outside in the tarped tent, and I don't know where in the shop we can put it when we bring it back in.
